1→package router
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"io/fs"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"sync/atomic"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler"
    12→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler/extension"
    13→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler/transport"
    14→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/playground"
    15→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    16→	chimiddleware "github.com/go-chi/chi/v5/middleware"
    17→	"github.com/go-chi/httprate"
    18→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    19→
    20→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/generated"
    21→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/resolver"
    22→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/auth"
    23→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/config"
    24→	kapowhandler "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/handler"
    25→	kapowmcp "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/mcp"
    26→	kapowmiddleware "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/middleware"
    27→	kapowotel "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/otel"
    28→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/preference"
    29→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    30→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/user"
    31→)
    32→
    33→// Deps holds the dependencies needed to construct the router.
    34→type Deps struct {
    35→	Config            *config.Config
    36→	DB                *pgxpool.Pool
    37→	Validator         auth.Validator
    38→	Search            *search.Registry
    39→	UserService       *user.Service
    40→	PreferenceService *preference.Service
    41→
    42→	// HTTPDetailConfig holds the hot-reloadable HTTP debug logging config.
    43→	// A future admin endpoint can call .Store() to change settings at runtime.
    44→	// If nil, New() initialises it from Config.
    45→	HTTPDetailConfig *atomic.Pointer[kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetailConfig]
    46→
    47→	// PrivateCIDRs restricts /private/* routes to these IP ranges.
    48→	PrivateCIDRs []*net.IPNet
    49→
    50→	// UI holds the embedded SPA filesystem. When non-nil, unmatched routes
    51→	// serve the SPA (static files + index.html fallback).
    52→	UI fs.FS
    53→}
    54→
    55→// New constructs the chi router with all middleware, routes, and handlers.
    56→func New(deps Deps) http.Handler {
    57→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    58→
    59→	// Initialise hot-reloadable HTTP detail config if not provided.
    60→	if deps.HTTPDetailConfig == nil {
    61→		deps.HTTPDetailConfig = &atomic.Pointer[kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetailConfig]{}
    62→		deps.HTTPDetailConfig.Store(kapowmiddleware.NewHTTPDetailConfig(deps.Config))
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	// Global middleware (applied to every request)
    66→	if deps.Config.OTelEnabled {
    67→		r.Use(kapowotel.Middleware("kapow-api"))
    68→	}
    69→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.RequestID)
    70→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Logging)
    71→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Recovery)
    72→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetail(deps.HTTPDetailConfig))
    73→	r.Use(chimiddleware.RealIP)
    74→	r.Use(chimiddleware.StripSlashes)
    75→
    76→	// Build gqlgen server
    77→	gqlSrv := handler.New(generated.NewExecutableSchema(generated.Config{
    78→		Resolvers: &resolver.Resolver{DB: deps.DB, Search: deps.Search, Preference: deps.PreferenceService},
    79→	}))
    80→	gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
    81→	gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
    82→	if deps.Config.GraphQLIntrospection {
    83→		gqlSrv.Use(extension.Introspection{})
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	// GraphQL endpoint — optional auth (anonymous allowed, user provisioned if token present)
    87→	r.Route("/graphql", func(r chi.Router) {
    88→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
    89→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.EnsureUser(deps.UserService))
    90→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
    91→			r.Use(authRateLimitOverride(deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQLAuth))
    92→			r.Use(httprate.Limit(
    93→				deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQL,
    94→				time.Minute,
    95→				httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
    96→			))
    97→		}
    98→		r.Post("/", gqlSrv.ServeHTTP)
    99→		r.Get("/", gqlSrv.ServeHTTP)
   100→	})
   101→
   102→	// Dev-only mock auth endpoint — issues JWTs without Supabase
   103→	if deps.Config.Env == "development" {
   104→		r.Post("/dev/auth/login", kapowhandler.DevLogin(deps.Config.JWTSecret))
   105→		slog.Info("router: dev auth endpoint enabled at /dev/auth/login")
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// GraphQL Playground (dev only)
   109→	if deps.Config.GraphQLPlayground {
   110→		r.Get("/playground", playground.Handler("Kapow API", "/graphql"))
   111→		slog.Info("router: GraphQL Playground enabled at /playground")
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// CSV export endpoint — optional auth + rate limited
   115→	r.Route("/api/export", func(r chi.Router) {
   116→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
   117→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.EnsureUser(deps.UserService))
   118→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
   119→			r.Use(httprate.Limit(
   120→				deps.Config.RateLimitExport,
   121→				time.Minute,
   122→				httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
   123→			))
   124→		}
   125→		r.Get("/csv", kapowhandler.ExportCSV(deps.DB, deps.Search, deps.Config.MaxExportRows))
   126→	})
   127→
   128→	// Private (internal) routes — IP allowlisted
   129→	r.Route("/private", func(r chi.Router) {
   130→		if len(deps.PrivateCIDRs) > 0 {
   131→			r.Use(kapowmiddleware.IPAllowlist(deps.PrivateCIDRs))
   132→		}
   133→		r.Get("/healthcheck", kapowhandler.Health(deps.DB))
   134→		r.Get("/log-config", kapowhandler.LogConfig(deps.Config))
   135→		r.Put("/log-config", kapowhandler.LogConfig(deps.Config))
   136→	})
   137→
   138→	// MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint — AI client integration
   139→	// Protected with mandatory Auth middleware; unauthenticated requests
   140→	// receive 401 with WWW-Authenticate header per MCP OAuth 2.1 spec.
   141→	if deps.Config.MCPEnabled {
   142→		r.Route("/mcp", func(r chi.Router) {
   143→			r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Auth(deps.Validator))
   144→			if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
   145→				r.Use(httprate.Limit(
   146→					deps.Config.RateLimitMCP,
   147→					time.Minute,
   148→					httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
   149→				))
   150→			}
   151→			r.Handle("/*", kapowmcp.NewHandler(deps.DB, deps.Search))
   152→		})
   153→		slog.Info("router: MCP endpoint enabled at /mcp")
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	// OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata (RFC 9728)
   157→	r.Get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", kapowhandler.ProtectedResourceMetadata(deps.Config))
   158→
   159→	// Embedded SPA — serve static files and fall back to index.html
   160→	if deps.UI != nil {
   161→		r.NotFound(kapowhandler.SPAHandler(deps.UI).ServeHTTP)
   162→		slog.Info("router: embedded SPA enabled")
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	slog.Info("router: initialized",
   166→		"graphql_introspection", deps.Config.GraphQLIntrospection,
   167→		"graphql_playground", deps.Config.GraphQLPlayground,
   168→		"mcp_enabled", deps.Config.MCPEnabled,
   169→		"rate_limit_enabled", deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled,
   170→		"embedded_ui", deps.UI != nil,
   171→	)
   172→
   173→	return r
   174→}
   175→
   176→// authOrIPKeyFunc keys by user subject if authenticated, otherwise by client IP.
   177→func authOrIPKeyFunc(r *http.Request) (string, error) {
   178→	if claims := auth.GetClaims(r.Context()); claims != nil {
   179→		return "user:" + claims.Subject, nil
   180→	}
   181→	return "ip:" + r.RemoteAddr, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→// authRateLimitOverride returns middleware that raises the httprate request limit
   185→// for authenticated users by setting WithRequestLimit in the request context.
   186→func authRateLimitOverride(authLimit int) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
   187→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
   188→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   189→			if auth.GetClaims(r.Context()) != nil {
   190→				r = r.WithContext(httprate.WithRequestLimit(r.Context(), authLimit))
   191→			}
   192→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
   193→		})
   194→	}
   195→}
   196→

1→package config
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"strings"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/caarlos0/env/v11"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
    13→type Config struct {
    14→	Host string `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    15→	Port int    `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    16→
    17→	DatabaseURL string `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    18→
    19→	JWTSecret    string `env:"JWT_SECRET"` //nolint:gosec // G117: config field name, not a hardcoded secret
    20→	JWTPublicKey string `env:"JWT_PUBLIC_KEY"`
    21→	JWTIssuer    string `env:"JWT_ISSUER"`
    22→	JWTAudience  string `env:"JWT_AUDIENCE"`
    23→
    24→	OIDCDiscoveryURL string `env:"OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL"`
    25→
    26→	LogLevel  string `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    27→	LogFormat string `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    28→
    29→	Env string `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    30→
    31→	CORSOrigins string `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    32→
    33→	GraphQLPlayground    bool `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    34→	GraphQLIntrospection bool `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"true"`
    35→
    36→	MCPEnabled  bool `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    37→	OTelEnabled bool `env:"OTEL_ENABLED" envDefault:"false"`
    38→
    39→	ShutdownTimeout time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
    40→
    41→	MaxExportRows int `env:"MAX_EXPORT_ROWS" envDefault:"500"`
    42→
    43→	// Rate limiting
    44→	RateLimitEnabled     bool   `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    45→	RateLimitGraphQL     int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    46→	RateLimitGraphQLAuth int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    47→	RateLimitExport      int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT" envDefault:"10"`
    48→	RateLimitMCP         int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    49→	PrivateAllowlistCIDRs string `env:"PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS" envDefault:"127.0.0.0/8,::1/128"`
    50→
    51→	// HTTP debug logging
    52→	HTTPDebugRequestHeaders  string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS" envDefault:"none"`
    53→	HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS" envDefault:"Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent"`
    54→	HTTPDebugRedactSensitive bool   `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE" envDefault:"true"`
    55→	HTTPDebugResponseMode    string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE" envDefault:"none"`
    56→	HTTPDebugBodyMaxBytes    int    `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES" envDefault:"4096"`
    57→
    58→	// logLevelVar holds the dynamic log level. It is initialized by LogHandler
    59→	// and can be updated at runtime to change the global log level without a restart.
    60→	logLevelVar slog.LevelVar
    61→
    62→	// swappableWriter is the log output target, swappable at runtime.
    63→	swappableWriter *SwappableWriter
    64→
    65→	// categoryHandler wraps the slog handler with per-category level overrides.
    66→	categoryHandler *CategoryHandler
    67→}
    68→
    69→// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    70→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    71→	cfg := &Config{}
    72→	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    73→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    74→	}
    75→	return cfg, nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    79→func (c *Config) Addr() string {
    80→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", c.Host, c.Port)
    81→}
    82→
    83→// LevelTrace is a custom log level below DEBUG for very detailed diagnostic
    84→// output. Usage: logger.Log(ctx, config.LevelTrace, "msg", "key", val)
    85→const LevelTrace = slog.Level(-8)
    86→
    87→// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    88→func ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    89→	switch s {
    90→	case "trace":
    91→		return LevelTrace
    92→	case "debug":
    93→		return slog.LevelDebug
    94→	case "warn":
    95→		return slog.LevelWarn
    96→	case "error":
    97→		return slog.LevelError
    98→	default:
    99→		return slog.LevelInfo
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// LogHandler returns an slog.Handler configured per LOG_LEVEL and LOG_FORMAT.
   104→// The handler uses a dynamic LevelVar so the log level can be changed at
   105→// runtime via LogLevelVar().Set() without recreating the handler. Output is
   106→// routed through a SwappableWriter and wrapped with a CategoryHandler for
   107→// per-category level overrides.
   108→func (c *Config) LogHandler() slog.Handler {
   109→	c.logLevelVar.Set(ParseLogLevel(c.LogLevel))
   110→	c.swappableWriter = NewSwappableWriter()
   111→
   112→	opts := &slog.HandlerOptions{
   113→		Level:     &c.logLevelVar,
   114→		AddSource: c.Env == "development",
   115→		ReplaceAttr: func(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {
   116→			if a.Key == slog.LevelKey {
   117→				if level, ok := a.Value.Any().(slog.Level); ok && level == LevelTrace {
   118→					a.Value = slog.StringValue("TRACE")
   119→				}
   120→			}
   121→			return a
   122→		},
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	var inner slog.Handler
   126→	if c.LogFormat == "text" {
   127→		inner = slog.NewTextHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   128→	} else {
   129→		inner = slog.NewJSONHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	c.categoryHandler = NewCategoryHandler(inner, &c.logLevelVar)
   133→	return c.categoryHandler
   134→}
   135→
   136→// LogLevelVar returns the dynamic log level variable. Callers can change
   137→// the active log level at runtime by calling Set() on the returned LevelVar.
   138→func (c *Config) LogLevelVar() *slog.LevelVar {
   139→	return &c.logLevelVar
   140→}
   141→
   142→// LogWriter returns the swappable writer used for log output.
   143→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   144→func (c *Config) LogWriter() *SwappableWriter {
   145→	return c.swappableWriter
   146→}
   147→
   148→// LogCategoryHandler returns the category handler for per-category level overrides.
   149→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   150→func (c *Config) LogCategoryHandler() *CategoryHandler {
   151→	return c.categoryHandler
   152→}
   153→
   154→// HTTPDebugEnabled reports whether any HTTP debug logging is configured.
   155→func (c *Config) HTTPDebugEnabled() bool {
   156→	return c.HTTPDebugRequestHeaders != "none" || c.HTTPDebugResponseMode != "none"
   157→}
   158→
   159→// ParsedSelectedHeaders splits the comma-separated HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders
   160→// into a trimmed slice of header names.
   161→func (c *Config) ParsedSelectedHeaders() []string {
   162→	raw := strings.Split(c.HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders, ",")
   163→	out := make([]string, 0, len(raw))
   164→	for _, h := range raw {
   165→		h = strings.TrimSpace(h)
   166→		if h != "" {
   167→			out = append(out, h)
   168→		}
   169→	}
   170→	return out
   171→}
   172→

    10→// Service manages user provisioning in the subscription schema.

    19→
    20→// EnsureUser idempotently creates a personal team and team membership for the
    21→// given user. If the user already has a team_member row, this is a no-op.
    22→func (s *Service) EnsureUser(ctx context.Context, userID string, email string) error {
    23→	const query = `
    24→		WITH new_team AS (
    25→			INSERT INTO subscription.team (id, name)
    26→			SELECT gen_random_uuid()::varchar(62), $2
    27→			WHERE NOT EXISTS (
    28→				SELECT 1 FROM subscription.team_member WHERE user_id = $1
    29→			)
    30→			RETURNING id
    31→		)
    32→		INSERT INTO subscription.team_member (id, team_id, user_id, role)
    33→		SELECT gen_random_uuid()::varchar(62), new_team.id, $1, 'OWNER'::subscription.role
    34→		FROM new_team
    35→		ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO NOTHING`
    36→
    37→	_, err := s.pool.Exec(ctx, query, userID, email)
    38→	if err != nil {
    39→		return fmt.Errorf("user: ensure user %s: %w", userID, err)
    40→	}
    41→	return nil
    42→}

1→/**
     2→ * Strict GraphQL utilities — re-exports urql hooks with types that reject raw strings.
     3→ *
     4→ * Import useQuery/useMutation from here instead of 'urql'. Raw string queries
     5→ * bypass gql.tada's compile-time schema validation, which has caused production
     6→ * bugs (e.g. querying fields that don't exist in the schema).
     7→ *
     8→ * These wrappers require a parsed DocumentNode (has 'kind' and 'definitions'),
     9→ * which gql.tada's graphql() returns but raw strings do not satisfy.
    10→ *
    11→ * Usage:
    12→ *   import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
    13→ *   import { useQuery, useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'
    14→ *
    15→ *   const MyQuery = graphql(`query MyQuery { ... }`)
    16→ *   const [result] = useQuery({ query: MyQuery })
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  useQuery as _useQuery,
    21→  useMutation as _useMutation,
    22→  type UseQueryResponse,
    23→  type UseMutationResponse,
    24→  type AnyVariables,
    25→  type OperationContext,
    26→  type RequestPolicy,
    27→} from 'urql'
    28→
    29→// A parsed GraphQL document has { kind: 'Document', definitions: [...] }.
    30→// gql.tada's graphql() returns this at runtime. Raw strings don't have these
    31→// properties, so TypeScript rejects them.
    32→interface ParsedDocument {
    33→  readonly kind: string
    34→  readonly definitions: readonly unknown[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→interface StrictQueryArgs<Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables> {
    38→  query: ParsedDocument
    39→  variables?: Variables
    40→  requestPolicy?: RequestPolicy
    41→  context?: Partial<OperationContext>
    42→  pause?: boolean
    43→}
    44→
    45→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    46→export function useQuery<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    47→  args: StrictQueryArgs<Variables>,
    48→): UseQueryResponse<Data, Variables> {
    49→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    50→  return _useQuery(args as any)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    54→export function useMutation<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    55→  query: ParsedDocument,
    56→): UseMutationResponse<Data, Variables> {
    57→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    58→  return _useMutation(query as any)
    59→}

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"flag"
     6→	"fmt"
     7→	"log/slog"
     8→	"os"
     9→	"os/signal"
    10→	"strings"
    11→	"syscall"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow/internal/config"
    14→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow/internal/dbutil"
    15→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/kapow/internal/fetch"
    16→)
    17→
    18→func main() {
    19→	if len(os.Args) < 2 {
    20→		printUsage()
    21→		os.Exit(1)
    22→	}
    23→
    24→	cmd := os.Args[1]
    25→
    26→	// Handle help and sources before flag parsing.
    27→	switch cmd {
    28→	case "-h", "--help", "help":
    29→		printUsage()
    30→		return
    31→	case "sources":
    32→		printSources()
    33→		return
    34→	}
    35→
    36→	cfg := &config.Config{}
    37→
    38→	// Check for a registered importer first.
    39→	if imp := Get(cmd); imp != nil {
    40→		fs := flag.NewFlagSet(cmd, flag.ExitOnError)
    41→		cfg.RegisterFlags(fs)
    42→		imp.RegisterFlags(fs)
    43→		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[2:]); err != nil {
    44→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: %v\n", err)
    45→			os.Exit(1)
    46→		}
    47→
    48→		setupLogging(cfg)
    49→		if err := runImporter(imp, cfg); err != nil {
    50→			slog.Error("import failed", "importer", cmd, "error", err)
    51→			os.Exit(1)
    52→		}
    53→		return
    54→	}
    55→
    56→	// Built-in commands.
    57→	switch cmd {
    58→	case "run-all":
    59→		fs := flag.NewFlagSet("run-all", flag.ExitOnError)
    60→		cfg.RegisterFlags(fs)
    61→		skipFlag := fs.String("skip", "", "comma-separated importers to skip")
    62→		onlyFlag := fs.String("only", "", "comma-separated importers to run (exclusive)")
    63→		if err := fs.Parse(os.Args[2:]); err != nil {
    64→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "error: %v\n", err)
    65→			os.Exit(1)
    66→		}
    67→
    68→		setupLogging(cfg)
    69→
    70→		skip := parseCSV(*skipFlag)
    71→		only := parseCSV(*onlyFlag)
    72→
    73→		if err := runAll(cfg, skip, only); err != nil {
    74→			slog.Error("run-all failed", "error", err)
    75→			os.Exit(1)
    76→		}
    77→	default:
    78→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "unknown command: %s\n\n", cmd)
    79→		printUsage()
    80→		os.Exit(1)
    81→	}
    82→}
    83→
    84→func runImporter(imp Importer, cfg *config.Config) error {
    85→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
    86→	defer cancel()
    87→
    88→	if cfg.DryRun {
    89→		slog.Info("dry-run mode enabled — no database writes")
    90→		fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
    91→		return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, nil, fetcher)
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
    95→		return fmt.Errorf("kapow: DATABASE_URL is required (set via --database-url or env)")
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
    99→	if err != nil {
   100→		return err
   101→	}
   102→	defer pool.Close()
   103→
   104→	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   105→	return imp.Run(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher)
   106→}
   107→
   108→func runAll(cfg *config.Config, skip, only map[string]bool) error {
   109→	ctx, cancel := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   110→	defer cancel()
   111→
   112→	if cfg.DatabaseURL == "" {
   113→		return fmt.Errorf("kapow: DATABASE_URL is required")
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	pool, err := dbutil.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		return err
   119→	}
   120→	defer pool.Close()
   121→
   122→	fetcher := fetch.New(cfg.CacheDir)
   123→
   124→	if err := RunAll(ctx, cfg, pool, fetcher, skip, only); err != nil {
   125→		return err
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	slog.Info("refreshing materialized views")
   129→	return dbutil.RefreshMaterializedViews(ctx, pool)
   130→}
   131→
   132→func setupLogging(cfg *config.Config) {
   133→	level := cfg.SlogLevel()
   134→	handler := slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: level})
   135→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(handler))
   136→}
   137→
   138→func parseCSV(s string) map[string]bool {
   139→	m := make(map[string]bool)
   140→	if s == "" {
   141→		return m
   142→	}
   143→	for _, v := range strings.Split(s, ",") {
   144→		v = strings.TrimSpace(v)
   145→		if v != "" {
   146→			m[v] = true
   147→		}
   148→	}
   149→	return m
   150→}
   151→
   152→func printUsage() {
   153→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `kapow — Kapow data pipeline tool
   154→
   155→Usage: kapow <command> [flags]
   156→
   157→Commands:
   158→  fetch <source>   Download and archive data from a source
   159→  fetch all        Download everything
   160→  import <source>  Import from archive into DB
   161→  import all       Import all in dependency order
   162→  run-all          Fetch all + import all in dependency order
   163→  sources          Print data source documentation
   164→
   165→Available sources:
   166→`)
   167→	for _, imp := range registry {
   168→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  %-16s %s\n", imp.Name(), imp.Description())
   169→	}
   170→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, `
   171→Global flags:
   172→  --database-url   PostgreSQL DSN (default: $DATABASE_URL)
   173→  --cache-dir      Download cache dir (default: ~/.cache/kapow)
   174→  --log-level      debug|info|warn|error (default: info)
   175→  --dry-run        Parse and validate without writing to DB
   176→`)
   177→}
   178→
   179→func printSources() {
   180→	fmt.Println("See DATA_SOURCES.md in this directory for complete data source documentation.")
   181→}
   182→

1→package main
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"context"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"os"
     7→	"os/signal"
     8→	"syscall"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5"
    11→	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/auth"
    14→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/config"
    15→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/db"
    16→	kapowmiddleware "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/middleware"
    17→	kapowotel "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/otel"
    18→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/preference"
    19→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/router"
    20→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    21→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/server"
    22→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/user"
    23→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/version"
    24→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/ui"
    25→)
    26→
    27→func main() {
    28→	// 1. Load .env (no-op if file is absent — dev only)
    29→	_ = godotenv.Load()
    30→
    31→	// 2. Parse and validate configuration
    32→	cfg, err := config.Load()
    33→	if err != nil {
    34→		slog.Error("failed to load config", "error", err)
    35→		os.Exit(1)
    36→	}
    37→
    38→	// 3. Configure structured logger
    39→	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(cfg.LogHandler()))
    40→	slog.Info("starting kapow api", "version", version.Version, "build_time", version.BuildTime, "env", cfg.Env)
    41→
    42→	// 4. Initialize OpenTelemetry (opt-in via OTEL_ENABLED)
    43→	ctx := context.Background()
    44→	mp, tp, err := kapowotel.Init(ctx, kapowotel.Config{
    45→		Enabled:     cfg.OTelEnabled,
    46→		ServiceName: "kapow-api",
    47→		Version:     version.Version,
    48→		Environment: cfg.Env,
    49→	})
    50→	if err != nil {
    51→		slog.Error("failed to initialize OpenTelemetry", "error", err)
    52→		os.Exit(1)
    53→	}
    54→	defer func() {
    55→		if err := kapowotel.Shutdown(ctx, mp, tp); err != nil {
    56→			slog.Error("otel shutdown error", "error", err)
    57→		}
    58→	}()
    59→	if cfg.OTelEnabled {
    60→		slog.Info("otel: initialized", "service", "kapow-api")
    61→	}
    62→
    63→	// 5. Connect to database
    64→	pool, err := db.Connect(ctx, cfg.DatabaseURL)
    65→	if err != nil {
    66→		slog.Error("failed to connect to database", "error", err)
    67→		os.Exit(1) //nolint:gocritic // fatal startup error; OS reclaims resources
    68→	}
    69→	defer pool.Close()
    70→
    71→	// 6. Select JWT validator
    72→	var jwtOpts []jwt.ParserOption
    73→	if cfg.JWTIssuer != "" {
    74→		jwtOpts = append(jwtOpts, jwt.WithIssuer(cfg.JWTIssuer))
    75→	}
    76→	if cfg.JWTAudience != "" {
    77→		jwtOpts = append(jwtOpts, jwt.WithAudience(cfg.JWTAudience))
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	var validator auth.Validator
    81→	if cfg.JWTPublicKey != "" {
    82→		validator, err = auth.NewRSAValidator(cfg.JWTPublicKey, jwtOpts...)
    83→		if err != nil {
    84→			slog.Error("failed to create RSA JWT validator", "error", err)
    85→			os.Exit(1) //nolint:gocritic // fatal startup error; OS reclaims resources
    86→		}
    87→		slog.Info("auth: using RS256 JWT validator")
    88→	} else {
    89→		validator, err = auth.NewHMACValidator(cfg.JWTSecret, jwtOpts...)
    90→		if err != nil {
    91→			slog.Error("failed to create HMAC JWT validator — set JWT_PUBLIC_KEY or JWT_SECRET", "error", err)
    92→			os.Exit(1) //nolint:gocritic // fatal startup error; OS reclaims resources
    93→		}
    94→		slog.Info("auth: using HS256 JWT validator")
    95→	}
    96→
    97→	// 7. Parse private endpoint CIDR allowlist
    98→	privateCIDRs, err := kapowmiddleware.ParseCIDRs(cfg.PrivateAllowlistCIDRs)
    99→	if err != nil {
   100→		slog.Error("failed to parse PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS", "error", err)
   101→		os.Exit(1) //nolint:gocritic // fatal startup error; OS reclaims resources
   102→	}
   103→	slog.Info("private endpoint allowlist configured", "cidrs", cfg.PrivateAllowlistCIDRs)
   104→
   105→	// 8. Build router
   106→	userSvc := user.NewService(pool)
   107→	prefSvc := preference.NewService(pool)
   108→
   109→	// Resolve embedded UI assets (nil when built without -tags ui).
   110→	uiFS := ui.Assets()
   111→	if uiFS != nil {
   112→		slog.Info("embedded UI assets loaded")
   113→	}
   114→
   115→	r := router.New(router.Deps{
   116→		Config:            cfg,
   117→		DB:                pool,
   118→		Validator:         validator,
   119→		Search:            search.NewRegistry(),
   120→		UserService:       userSvc,
   121→		PreferenceService: prefSvc,
   122→		PrivateCIDRs:      privateCIDRs,
   123→		UI:                uiFS,
   124→	})
   125→
   126→	// 9. Build HTTP server
   127→	srv := server.New(cfg, r)
   128→
   129→	// 10. Listen for OS signals
   130→	sigCh := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
   131→	signal.Notify(sigCh, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
   132→
   133→	// 11. Start server in background goroutine
   134→	go func() {
   135→		slog.Info("server: listening", "addr", cfg.Addr())
   136→		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil {
   137→			// ErrServerClosed is expected during graceful shutdown
   138→			slog.Info("server: stopped", "reason", err)
   139→		}
   140→	}()
   141→
   142→	// 12. Block until signal received
   143→	sig := <-sigCh
   144→	slog.Info("signal received", "signal", sig)
   145→
   146→	// 13. Graceful shutdown
   147→	if err := server.Shutdown(srv, cfg.ShutdownTimeout); err != nil {
   148→		slog.Error("server: shutdown error", "error", err)
   149→		os.Exit(1)
   150→	}
   151→}
   152→

     5→interface AuthState {
     6→  session: Session | null
     7→  user: User | null

     9→
    10→  initialize: () => Promise<void>
    11→  setSession: (session: Session | null) => void
    12→  signOut: () => Promise<void>
    13→  getToken: () => string | undefined
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const useAuthStore = create<AuthState>()((set, get) => ({
    17→  session: null,
    18→  user: null,
    19→  loading: true,
    20→
    21→  initialize: async () => {
    22→    const {
    23→      data: { session },
    24→    } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
    25→    set({ session, user: session?.user ?? null, loading: false })
    26→
    27→    supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange(
    28→      (_event: AuthChangeEvent, session: Session | null) => {
    29→        set({ session, user: session?.user ?? null })
    30→      },
    31→    )
    32→  },
    33→
    34→  setSession: (session: Session | null) => {
    35→    set({ session, user: session?.user ?? null })
    36→  },
    37→
    38→  signOut: async () => {
    39→    await supabase.auth.signOut()
    40→    set({ session: null, user: null })
    41→  },
    42→
    43→  getToken: () => {
    44→    return get().session?.access_token
    45→  },
    46→}))

content.text:
I have a thorough understanding of the codebase now. Let me compile the implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: User Feedback System for Kapow

### 1. Database Changes

The existing `internal.user_feedback` table already has the right shape:

```sql
CREATE TABLE "internal"."user_feedback" (
    "id" VARCHAR(62) NOT NULL,
    "created" TIMESTAMPTZ(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "user_id" VARCHAR(64),
    "user_email" VARCHAR(255),
    "data" JSONB NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT "user_feedback_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);
```

**Migration needed** (new file `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`):

Add a `github_issue_url` column to track sync status, and an index for efficient "unsynced" queries:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

This is better than putting `github_issue_url` inside the JSONB `data` column because it enables a clean `WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL` index for the batch sync query.

---

### 2. GraphQL Schema

Create a new file `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`:

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory {
  BUG
  FEATURE
  GENERAL
}

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  """Optional email — only used for anonymous submissions."""
  email: String
  """Browser user-agent string (auto-populated by frontend)."""
  userAgent: String
  """Page URL where feedback was submitted."""
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult {
  id: String!
  success: Boolean!
}

extend type Mutation {
  """
  Submit user feedback. Works for both authenticated and anonymous users.
  If authenticated, user_id and email are taken from the JWT.
  """
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

The `FeedbackCategory` enum does not need a custom model binding in `gqlgen.yml` -- the generated Go string constants are fine.

---

### 3. Go Service Layer (`api/internal/feedback/`)

Create a new package `api/internal/feedback/` following the `preference` service pattern:

**`api/internal/feedback/service.go`**:

- `Service` struct holding `*pgxpool.Pool` (needs `Exec`, so use `pgxpool.Pool` directly, same as `user.Service` and `preference.Service`)
- `NewService(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service`
- `Submit(ctx context.Context, input SubmitInput) (string, error)` -- generates a `gen_random_uuid()` ID, marshals category/subject/description/userAgent/pageUrl into the JSONB `data` column, populates `user_id` and `user_email` from caller-provided values
- Input struct: `SubmitInput { Category, Subject, Description, Email, UserAgent, PageUrl, UserID, UserEmail string }`
- Validation: subject and description must be non-empty, subject max 200 chars, description max 5000 chars, email (if provided) basic format check

**Config addition** (`api/internal/config/config.go`):

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

**Resolver wiring**:

1. Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` to `Resolver` struct in `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go`
2. Wire it in `router.go` when building the `resolver.Resolver{}`
3. Implement `submitFeedback` resolver in the auto-generated `api/graph/resolver/feedback.resolvers.go`:
   - Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` -- if present, use `claims.Subject` and `claims.Email`
   - If not authenticated and anonymous feedback is disabled (check config via a flag on the Resolver or a dedicated field), return error
   - If not authenticated, use the `email` from input (optional)
   - Call `feedback.Service.Submit()`

**Config access in resolver**: The resolver currently does not have access to config. Two approaches:
- (A) Add `Config *config.Config` to the `Resolver` struct (simplest, matches how router.Deps already has Config)
- (B) Add just `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool` to the `Resolver` struct

Approach (A) is better -- it is forward-looking and the Resolver struct is internal to the resolver package.

**Rate limiting**: The GraphQL endpoint already has rate limiting (60/min anonymous, 120/min authenticated). A dedicated feedback-specific rate limit is probably overkill initially since the global rate limit applies. If needed later, it can be added as resolver-level logic (track last submission time per IP/user in memory or Redis).

---

### 4. GitHub Sync Tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Create a new standalone Go module at `tools/feedbacksync/` following the existing `tools/samplesql` pattern:

**Go GitHub Client Decision**: Use `google/go-github` (REST API v3).

Rationale:
- **`google/go-github`**: 11k+ GitHub stars, actively maintained by Google, covers 100% of the Issues API, excellent documentation. REST is simpler for creating issues with labels. MIT license.
- **`shurcooL/githubv4`**: GraphQL v4 -- more efficient for complex queries but overkill for "create an issue with labels". Less popular, less actively maintained.
- **`gh` CLI subprocess**: No Go dependency, but fragile (parsing stdout), harder to test, requires `gh` installed in production.
- **Raw HTTP**: Maximum flexibility but requires manual auth handling, pagination, error parsing. Not worth the effort.

`google/go-github` is the clear winner for this use case.

**Module structure**:

```
tools/feedbacksync/
  go.mod          (module github.com/ketang/kapow/tools/feedbacksync)
  go.sum
  main.go         (CLI entry point with flag parsing)
  sync.go         (core sync logic)
  sync_test.go    (unit tests with mocked GitHub client)
```

**CLI interface**:

```bash
cd tools/feedbacksync
go run . --database-url "$DATABASE_URL" --github-token "$GITHUB_TOKEN" --repo "ketang/kapow" --dry-run
```

Flags:
- `--database-url` (or `DATABASE_URL` env): PostgreSQL connection string
- `--github-token` (or `GITHUB_TOKEN` env): GitHub personal access token with `repo` scope
- `--repo`: GitHub owner/repo (default: `ketang/kapow`)
- `--dry-run`: Print what would be created without making API calls
- `--batch-size`: Max issues to create per run (default: 50, safety limit)
- `--log-level`: debug/info/warn/error

**Sync logic** (`sync.go`):

1. Query `SELECT id, created, user_id, user_email, data FROM internal.user_feedback WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL ORDER BY created LIMIT $1`
2. For each row:
   a. Parse the JSONB `data` to extract category, subject, description, userAgent, pageUrl
   b. Map category to label: `feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`
   c. Build GitHub issue title: `[Feedback] {subject}` (truncated to 256 chars)
   d. Build issue body with Markdown template including all fields + DB record ID
   e. Create issue via `google/go-github` with appropriate labels
   f. Update the DB row: `UPDATE internal.user_feedback SET github_issue_url = $1 WHERE id = $2`
3. Log summary: N issues created, N errors

**Label auto-creation**: On first run, ensure the three labels exist (`feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`) via the GitHub API. Use create-or-update pattern (409 Conflict = already exists, which is fine).

**Makefile targets** (add to root `Makefile`):

```makefile
feedbacksync-build:
    cd tools/feedbacksync && go build -o ../../bin/feedbacksync .

feedbacksync-test:
    cd tools/feedbacksync && go test ./...
```

---

### 5. Frontend Feedback Form

**New files**:

```
web/src/components/feedback/
  FeedbackButton.tsx       (floating action button -- persistent UI trigger)
  FeedbackButton.test.tsx
  FeedbackModal.tsx        (the modal with form)
  FeedbackModal.test.tsx
```

**FeedbackButton** component:
- A floating action button (Mantine `ActionIcon` or `Button`) positioned fixed at bottom-right
- Uses Mantine's `Affix` component for fixed positioning
- Renders a message/feedback icon (from `lucide-react`, which the project already uses)
- Opens FeedbackModal via `useDisclosure` hook
- Placed in `App.tsx` alongside `<Footer />`

**FeedbackModal** component:
- Uses Mantine `Modal` component (consistent with `PreferencesModal` pattern)
- Form fields:
  - **Category**: `Select` component with options Bug / Feature Request / General (required)
  - **Subject**: `TextInput` (required, max 200 chars)
  - **Description**: `Textarea` (required, max 5000 chars)
  - **Email**: `TextInput` (optional, only shown when `!session` from `useAuthStore`)
- Uses `@mantine/form` for validation (same pattern as other forms in the project)
- GraphQL mutation via `gql.tada`:

```tsx
import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
import { useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'

const SubmitFeedbackMutation = graphql(`
  mutation SubmitFeedback($input: SubmitFeedbackInput!) {
    submitFeedback(input: $input) {
      id
      success
    }
  }
`)
```

- Auto-populates `userAgent` from `navigator.userAgent` and `pageUrl` from `window.location.href`
- Shows success notification via Mantine `notifications` after submission
- Clears form and closes modal on success
- Error state shown inline

**Integration in App.tsx**:
- Import and render `<FeedbackButton />` inside the root `<div>`, after `<Footer />`

---

### 6. Claude Code Integration Approach

This is a documented process rather than a custom tool. Create a document `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` describing the workflow:

**Manual triage workflow** (using `gh` CLI, which is already available):

1. List untriaged feedback issues:
   ```bash
   gh issue list --label "feedback:bug" --label "feedback:feature" --label "feedback:general" --state open
   ```

2. For each issue, Claude can:
   - Read the issue body to understand the feedback
   - Add priority labels (`priority:high`, `priority:low`)
   - For actionable items, create a corresponding beads issue:
     ```bash
     bd create --title "Fix: <summary>" --description "From user feedback: <link>"
     ```
   - Close the GitHub issue if it is a duplicate or not actionable, with a comment explaining why

3. Periodic triage can be triggered by the developer asking Claude: "Triage open feedback issues"

This approach is lightweight and does not require building a custom skill. It leverages Claude's existing ability to use `gh` and `bd` CLI tools.

---

### 7. Testing Strategy

**Backend unit tests** (`api/internal/feedback/service_test.go`):
- Validate input constraints (empty subject, too-long description, invalid email format)
- Verify JSONB data structure marshaling

**Backend integration test** (in resolver test file):
- `IntegrationSubmitFeedback_Authenticated` -- submit with JWT, verify user_id/user_email populated
- `IntegrationSubmitFeedback_Anonymous` -- submit without auth, verify email from input used
- `IntegrationSubmitFeedback_AnonymousDisabled` -- verify error when config flag is off

**Frontend unit tests**:
- `FeedbackButton.test.tsx` -- renders, opens modal on click
- `FeedbackModal.test.tsx` -- form validation (required fields, max lengths), email field visibility based on auth state, mutation called with correct input, success/error states

**GitHub sync tool tests** (`tools/feedbacksync/sync_test.go`):
- Mock the GitHub client interface to verify issue creation payload
- Mock DB rows to verify the query-create-update flow
- Test label mapping (category to label name)
- Test dry-run mode (no API calls)
- Test batch size limiting

**E2E test** (optional, can be deferred):
- Playwright test that opens feedback modal, fills form, submits, verifies success notification

---

### 8. Implementation Sequence

1. **Migration** -- add `github_issue_url` column (5 min)
2. **GraphQL schema** -- `feedback.graphql` (10 min)
3. **Feedback service** -- `api/internal/feedback/` (30 min)
4. **Resolver + wiring** -- resolver implementation + router.go + resolver.go changes (20 min)
5. **Config flag** -- `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED` (5 min)
6. **Backend tests** -- unit + integration (30 min)
7. **Frontend mutation + modal** -- `FeedbackModal.tsx` (45 min)
8. **Frontend button + integration** -- `FeedbackButton.tsx` + App.tsx (15 min)
9. **Frontend tests** -- (30 min)
10. **Schema sync** -- `make web-schema-sync` (2 min)
11. **Build verification** -- `make test-standard` + `pnpm build` + `pnpm lint` (5 min)
12. **GitHub sync tool** -- `tools/feedbacksync/` (60 min)
13. **Triage docs** -- `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` (15 min)
14. **Makefile targets** -- feedbacksync-build, feedbacksync-test (5 min)

Steps 1-11 form the core feature (a single cohesive PR). Steps 12-14 can be a separate PR for the GitHub sync tool.

---

### Critical Files for Implementation
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/internal/preference/service.go` - Pattern to follow for the feedback service (pgxpool, JSONB, constructor)
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go` - Pattern to follow for mutation resolver with auth check
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/internal/router/router.go` - Must add FeedbackService to Deps and wire into resolver
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/api/graph/schema/preferences.graphql` - Pattern to follow for extending Mutation type in new SDL file
- `/home/ketan/project/kapow/web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx` - Pattern to follow for Mantine modal with form and GraphQL mutation

toolUseResult.prompt:
Design an implementation plan for a user feedback system in the Kapow project. Here's the context:

## Project Context
- **Kapow** is a college search app with a Go API (`api/`) using chi router and a React/TypeScript frontend (`web/`) using Mantine UI
- **GitHub repo**: `ketang/kapow`
- **Existing DB table**: `internal.user_feedback` with columns: id, created, user_id, user_email, data (JSONB)
- **Auth**: JWT-based with OptionalAuth middleware (allows anonymous requests)
- **Issue tracking**: Uses "beads" (JSONL-based) for dev work. GitHub Issues will be for user feedback only.
- **GraphQL**: Schema-first with gqlgen. Mutations defined in `api/graph/schema/` SDL files.
- **Router**: `api/internal/router/router.go` with chi, dependencies passed via `router.Deps` struct

## Requirements
1. **Frontend feedback form** (Mantine modal/drawer):
   - Categories: Bug, Feature Request, General
   - Fields: category (required), subject (required), description (required), email (optional, shown only for anonymous users)
   - Anonymous submissions allowed (with config flag to disable)
   - Accessible from a persistent UI element (e.g., footer link or floating button)

2. **Backend**:
   - GraphQL mutation `submitFeedback` that saves to `internal.user_feedback` table
   - Store category, subject, description, browser info, current page URL in JSONB `data` column
   - If user is authenticated, populate user_id and user_email from JWT claims
   - Rate limiting to prevent spam

3. **GitHub sync** (batch):
   - A CLI command or tool that reads unsynced feedback from DB and creates GitHub Issues
   - Issues labeled by category (`feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`)
   - Issue body includes all feedback data + link back to DB record
   - Tracks which feedback items have been synced (add `github_issue_url` to JSONB or a new column)
   - Uses a Go GitHub client library

4. **Claude Code integration**:
   - A workflow/skill or documented process for Claude to triage GitHub issues created from feedback
   - Claude analyzes the issue, adds labels, may create corresponding beads issues for actionable items
   - Uses `gh` CLI (already available)

## Key decisions made by user
- Anonymous allowed initially, easy to disable via config
- GitHub for user feedback only; beads stays for internal dev work
- Bug / Feature / General categories
- Store in DB first, then batch sync to GitHub

## Go GitHub client options to evaluate
- `google/go-github` (REST API v3)
- `shurcooL/githubv4` (GraphQL v4)
- Direct `gh` CLI subprocess calls
- Raw HTTP calls to GitHub API

Please design a detailed implementation plan covering:
1. Database changes (if any needed beyond existing table)
2. GraphQL schema additions
3. Go service layer for feedback
4. GitHub sync tool/command
5. Frontend feedback form component
6. Claude Code integration approach
7. Testing strategy
8. File list with specific paths

Consider that the project already has a `tools/` directory with standalone Go modules (kapow, samplesql), so the GitHub sync could be another tool there or a subcommand of the API.

I have a thorough understanding of the codebase now. Let me compile the implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: User Feedback System for Kapow

### 1. Database Changes

The existing `internal.user_feedback` table already has the right shape:

```sql
CREATE TABLE "internal"."user_feedback" (
    "id" VARCHAR(62) NOT NULL,
    "created" TIMESTAMPTZ(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "user_id" VARCHAR(64),
    "user_email" VARCHAR(255),
    "data" JSONB NOT NULL,
    CONSTRAINT "user_feedback_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);
```

**Migration needed** (new file `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`):

Add a `github_issue_url` column to track sync status, and an index for efficient "unsynced" queries:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

This is better than putting `github_issue_url` inside the JSONB `data` column because it enables a clean `WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL` index for the batch sync query.

---

### 2. GraphQL Schema

Create a new file `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`:

```graphql
  BUG
  FEATURE
  GENERAL
}

  category: FeedbackCategory!

  """Optional email — only used for anonymous submissions."""
  email: String
  """Browser user-agent string (auto-populated by frontend)."""
  userAgent: String
  """Page URL where feedback was submitted."""
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult {
  id: String!
  success: Boolean!
}

extend type Mutation {
  """
  Submit user feedback. Works for both authenticated and anonymous users.
  If authenticated, user_id and email are taken from the JWT.
  """
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

The `FeedbackCategory` enum does not need a custom model binding in `gqlgen.yml` -- the generated Go string constants are fine.

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

**Resolver wiring**:

1. Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` to `Resolver` struct in `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go`

   - Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` -- if present, use `claims.Subject` and `claims.Email`
   - If not authenticated and anonymous feedback is disabled (check config via a flag on the Resolver or a dedicated field), return error

   - Call `feedback.Service.Submit()`

**Config access in resolver**: The resolver currently does not have access to config. Two approaches:
- (A) Add `Config *config.Config` to the `Resolver` struct (simplest, matches how router.Deps already has Config)
- (B) Add just `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool` to the `Resolver` struct

Approach (A) is better -- it is forward-looking and the Resolver struct is internal to the resolver package.

**Rate limiting**: The GraphQL endpoint already has rate limiting (60/min anonymous, 120/min authenticated). A dedicated feedback-specific rate limit is probably overkill initially since the global rate limit applies. If needed later, it can be added as resolver-level logic (track last submission time per IP/user in memory or Redis).

---

### 4. GitHub Sync Tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Create a new standalone Go module at `tools/feedbacksync/` following the existing `tools/samplesql` pattern:

**Go GitHub Client Decision**: Use `google/go-github` (REST API v3).

Rationale:
- **`google/go-github`**: 11k+ GitHub stars, actively maintained by Google, covers 100% of the Issues API, excellent documentation. REST is simpler for creating issues with labels. MIT license.
- **`shurcooL/githubv4`**: GraphQL v4 -- more efficient for complex queries but overkill for "create an issue with labels". Less popular, less actively maintained.
- **`gh` CLI subprocess**: No Go dependency, but fragile (parsing stdout), harder to test, requires `gh` installed in production.
- **Raw HTTP**: Maximum flexibility but requires manual auth handling, pagination, error parsing. Not worth the effort.

`google/go-github` is the clear winner for this use case.


**Makefile targets** (add to root `Makefile`):

```makefile

    cd tools/feedbacksync && go build -o ../../bin/feedbacksync .

```

---

### 5. Frontend Feedback Form

**New files**:

```
web/src/components/feedback/
  FeedbackButton.tsx       (floating action button -- persistent UI trigger)
  FeedbackButton.test.tsx
  FeedbackModal.tsx        (the modal with form)
  FeedbackModal.test.tsx
```

**FeedbackButton** component:
- A floating action button (Mantine `ActionIcon` or `Button`) positioned fixed at bottom-right

  - **Category**: `Select` component with options Bug / Feature Request / General (required)

- Uses `@mantine/form` for validation (same pattern as other forms in the project)
- GraphQL mutation via `gql.tada`:

```tsx
import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
import { useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'

const SubmitFeedbackMutation = graphql(`
  mutation SubmitFeedback($input: SubmitFeedbackInput!) {
    submitFeedback(input: $input) {
      id
      success
```

**Integration in App.tsx**:

### 6. Claude Code Integration Approach

This is a documented process rather than a custom tool. Create a document `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` describing the workflow:

**Manual triage workflow** (using `gh` CLI, which is already available):

1. List untriaged feedback issues:


   - Close the GitHub issue if it is a duplicate or not actionable, with a comment explaining why

3. Periodic triage can be triggered by the developer asking Claude: "Triage open feedback issues"

This approach is lightweight and does not require building a custom skill. It leverages Claude's existing ability to use `gh` and `bd` CLI tools.

---

### 7. Testing Strategy

**Backend unit tests** (`api/internal/feedback/service_test.go`):

- Verify JSONB data structure marshaling

**Backend integration test** (in resolver test file):
- `IntegrationSubmitFeedback_Authenticated` -- submit with JWT, verify user_id/user_email populated

**GitHub sync tool tests** (`tools/feedbacksync/sync_test.go`):

---

### 8. Implementation Sequence

1. **Migration** -- add `github_issue_url` column (5 min)

input.content:
# Feedback Form + GitHub Issues Integration

## Context

Kapow has no user-facing way to submit feedback. Users need a simple form to
report bugs, request features, or send general comments. Feedback should flow
into GitHub Issues for visibility and triage. The existing beads tracker stays
for internal dev work; GitHub Issues handles user-reported feedback only.

The `internal.user_feedback` table already exists in the database with a
flexible JSONB `data` column — we build on top of it.

---

## Go GitHub Client Library

| Library | Stars | License | Pros | Cons | Fit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `google/go-github` | 11k+ | BSD-3 | Actively maintained by Google; full Issues API coverage; well-documented | REST only (v3); large dependency | Best fit — simple issue creation |
| `shurcooL/githubv4` | 1k+ | MIT | GraphQL v4; efficient for complex queries | Overkill for creating issues; less maintained | Over-engineered for this |
| `gh` CLI subprocess | N/A | N/A | No Go dependency; already installed | Fragile stdout parsing; hard to test | Fragile |
| Raw HTTP | N/A | N/A | Zero dependencies | Manual auth, pagination, error handling | Too much boilerplate |

**Recommendation**: `google/go-github` — proven, well-maintained, right level of abstraction.

---

## Architecture

```
User submits form
  → GraphQL mutation (submitFeedback)
  → Saved to internal.user_feedback table (JSONB)
  → Batch sync tool reads unsynced rows
  → Creates GitHub Issues with labels (feedback:bug, feedback:feature, feedback:general)
  → Claude triages via `gh` CLI + promotes actionable items to beads
```

---

## Implementation

### 1. Database migration (`api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`)

Add `github_issue_url` column + partial index for unsynced rows:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

### 2. GraphQL schema (`api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`)

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory { BUG  FEATURE  GENERAL }

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  email: String
  userAgent: String
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult { id: String!  success: Boolean! }

extend type Mutation {
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

### 3. Feedback service (`api/internal/feedback/service.go`)

- `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool` (follows `preference.Service` pattern)
- `Submit(ctx, input)` — validates, generates ID, marshals to JSONB, inserts
- Validation: subject required (max 200), description required (max 5000), email format if provided
- Pattern reference: `api/internal/preference/service.go`

### 4. Config flag (`api/internal/config/config.go`)

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

Document in `.env.example`.

### 5. Resolver + wiring

- Add `submitFeedback` resolver in `api/graph/resolver/` (auto-generated file after `make api-generate`)
- Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` — use user_id/email from JWT if present
- If not authenticated and `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=false`, return error
- Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` and `Config *config.Config` to `Resolver` struct
- Wire in `api/internal/router/router.go`
- Pattern reference: `api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go`

### 6. Frontend feedback form

**New files**:
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` — floating `Affix` button (bottom-right)
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` — Mantine Modal with form
- Tests alongside each component

**Form fields**: Category (Select), Subject (TextInput), Description (Textarea), Email (TextInput, only shown for anonymous users)

**Integration**: Render `<FeedbackButton />` in `App.tsx`. Uses `gql.tada` for the mutation.

Pattern reference: `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx`

### 7. GitHub sync tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Standalone Go module using `google/go-github`:

```
tools/feedbacksync/
  go.mod
  main.go       (CLI: --database-url, --github-token, --repo, --dry-run, --batch-size)
  sync.go       (query unsynced → create GH issue → update row with issue URL)
  sync_test.go
```

- Maps categories to labels: `feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`
- Auto-creates labels on first run
- Issue title: `[Feedback] {subject}`
- Issue body: Markdown with all fields + DB record ID
- Batch size default: 50

**Makefile targets**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test`

### 8. Claude Code triage workflow (`docs/specs/feedback-triage.md`)

Documented workflow using `gh` CLI:
- List open feedback issues by label
- Analyze, prioritize, add labels
- Promote actionable items to beads: `bd create --title "..." --description "From feedback: <url>"`
- Close duplicates/non-actionable with explanatory comment

---

## Key files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/internal/config/config.go` | Add `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled` field |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire `feedback.Service` + Config into resolver |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | Add `Feedback` + `Config` to Resolver struct |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | New file |
| `web/src/App.tsx` | Add `<FeedbackButton />` |
| `.env.example` | Document `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED` |
| `Makefile` | Add feedbacksync targets |

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql` | Add github_issue_url column |
| `api/internal/feedback/service.go` | Feedback service |
| `api/internal/feedback/service_test.go` | Unit tests |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | GraphQL schema |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` | Floating trigger button |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.test.tsx` | Button tests |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` | Modal with form |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx` | Modal tests |
| `tools/feedbacksync/` | GitHub sync tool (go.mod, main.go, sync.go, sync_test.go) |
| `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` | Triage workflow docs |

---

## Verification

1. `make api-generate` — codegen succeeds
2. `make test-standard` — all tests pass
3. `pnpm build && pnpm lint` — zero errors/warnings
4. Manual test: open feedback modal, submit form, verify row in `user_feedback` table
5. `cd tools/feedbacksync && go test ./...` — sync tool tests pass
6. Dry run: `go run . --dry-run` against dev DB — verify issue format
7. Live run: sync one feedback item to GitHub, verify issue created with correct labels

content.content:
Create a new issue (or multiple issues from markdown file)

Usage:
  bd create [title] [flags]

Aliases:
  create, new

Flags:
      --acceptance string       Acceptance criteria
      --agent-rig string        Agent's rig name (requires --type=agent)
      --append-notes string     Append to existing notes (with newline separator)
  -a, --assignee string         Assignee
      --body-file string        Read description from file (use - for stdin)
      --defer string            Defer until date (issue hidden from bd ready until then). Same formats as --due
      --deps strings            Dependencies in format 'type:id' or 'id' (e.g., 'discovered-from:bd-20,blocks:bd-15' or 'bd-20')
  -d, --description string      Issue description
      --design string           Design notes
      --dry-run                 Preview what would be created without actually creating
      --due string              Due date/time. Formats: +6h, +1d, +2w, tomorrow, next monday, 2025-01-15
      --ephemeral               Create as ephemeral (short-lived, subject to TTL compaction)
  -e, --estimate int            Time estimate in minutes (e.g., 60 for 1 hour)
      --event-actor string      Entity URI who caused this event (requires --type=event)
      --event-category string   Event category (e.g., patrol.muted, agent.started) (requires --type=event)
      --event-payload string    Event-specific JSON data (requires --type=event)
      --event-target string     Entity URI or bead ID affected (requires --type=event)
      --external-ref string     External reference (e.g., 'gh-9', 'jira-ABC')
  -f, --file string             Create multiple issues from markdown file
      --force                   Force creation even if prefix doesn't match database prefix
  -h, --help                    help for create
      --id string               Explicit issue ID (e.g., 'bd-42' for partitioning)
  -l, --labels strings          Labels (comma-separated)
      --metadata string         Set custom metadata (JSON string or @file.json to read from file)
      --mol-type string         Molecule type: swarm (multi-polecat), patrol (recurring ops), work (default)
      --no-inherit-labels       Don't inherit labels from parent issue
      --notes string            Additional notes
      --parent string           Parent issue ID for hierarchical child (e.g., 'bd-a3f8e9')
      --prefix string           Create issue in rig by prefix (e.g., --prefix bd- or --prefix bd or --prefix beads)
  -p, --priority string         Priority (0-4 or P0-P4, 0=highest) (default "2")
      --repo string             Target repository for issue (overrides auto-routing)

--silent                  Output only the issue ID (for scripting)
      --spec-id string          Link to specification document
      --stdin                   Read description from stdin (alias for --body-file -)
      --title string            Issue title (alternative to positional argument)
  -t, --type string             Issue type (bug|feature|task|epic|chore|decision); custom types require types.custom config; aliases: enhancement/feat→feature, dec/adr→decision (default "task")
      --validate                Validate description contains required sections for issue type
      --waits-for string        Spawner issue ID to wait for (creates waits-for dependency for fanout gate)
      --waits-for-gate string   Gate type: all-children (wait for all) or any-children (wait for first) (default "all-children")
      --wisp-type string        Wisp type for TTL-based compaction: heartbeat, ping, patrol, gc_report, recovery, error, escalation

Global Flags:
      --actor string              Actor name for audit trail (default: $BD_ACTOR, git user.name, $USER)
      --db string                 Database path (default: auto-discover .beads/*.db)
      --dolt-auto-commit string   Dolt auto-commit policy (off|on|batch). 'on': commit after each write. 'batch': defer commits to bd sync / bd dolt commit; uncommitted changes persist in the working set until then. SIGTERM/SIGHUP flush pending batch commits. Default: off. Override via config key dolt.auto-commit
      --json                      Output in JSON format
      --profile                   Generate CPU profile for performance analysis
  -q, --quiet                     Suppress non-essential output (errors only)
      --readonly                  Read-only mode: block write operations (for worker sandboxes)
      --sandbox                   Sandbox mode: disables auto-sync
  -v, --verbose                   Enable verbose/debug output

Thin vertical slice: a user can open a feedback modal, fill in category/subject/description, submit it, and see a success confirmation. The feedback is persisted in the database.

## Scope (end-to-end)
- **Migration**: Add `github_issue_url` column to `internal.user_feedback` (needed by sync slice, but cheap to include now)
- **GraphQL schema**: `feedback.graphql` with `SubmitFeedbackInput`, `FeedbackCategory` enum (BUG/FEATURE/GENERAL), `FeedbackResult`, `submitFeedback` mutation
- **Service**: `api/internal/feedback/service.go` — validates input, marshals to JSONB, inserts row. Follows `preference.Service` pattern
- **Config**: `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED` env var (default true) to toggle anonymous submissions
- **Resolver**: Wire service + config into resolver. Use `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` for user_id/email when authenticated; accept optional email for anonymous
- **Frontend**: `FeedbackButton.tsx` (floating Affix, bottom-right) + `FeedbackModal.tsx` (Mantine Modal with form). Category Select, Subject TextInput, Description Textarea, Email TextInput (anonymous only). Uses `gql.tada` mutation
- **Tests**: Service unit tests, frontend component tests, build + lint verification

## Key patterns to follow
- `api/internal/preference/service.go` — service with pgxpool
- `api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go` — mutation resolver with auth check
- `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx` — Mantine modal with form + GraphQL mutation

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Anonymous user can submit feedback with email
- [ ] Authenticated user can submit feedback (email auto-filled from JWT)
- [ ] Form validates required fields and shows errors
- [ ] Success notification appears after submission
- [ ] Row appears in `internal.user_feedback` with correct JSONB structure
- [ ] `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=false` blocks anonymous submissions
- [ ] `make test-standard` passes; `pnpm build && pnpm lint` clean
EOF
)" --estimate 120 --silent 2>&1

Thin vertical slice: a CLI tool reads unsynced feedback from the database and creates corresponding GitHub Issues with appropriate labels.

## Scope (end-to-end)
- **Tool**: `tools/feedbacksync/` — standalone Go module using `google/go-github`
- **CLI flags**: `--database-url`, `--github-token`, `--repo` (default: ketang/kapow), `--dry-run`, `--batch-size` (default: 50)
- **Sync logic**: Query rows where `github_issue_url IS NULL` → create GH issue with label (`feedback:bug`/`feedback:feature`/`feedback:general`) → update row with issue URL
- **Issue format**: Title `[Feedback] {subject}`, body with all fields + DB record ID
- **Label bootstrap**: Auto-create the three feedback labels on first run (idempotent)
- **Makefile**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test` targets
- **Tests**: Unit tests with mocked GitHub client; verify payload, label mapping, dry-run, batch limiting

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows what would be created without API calls
- [ ] Live run creates issues with correct labels and formatted body
- [ ] DB rows updated with `github_issue_url` after sync
- [ ] Re-running does not create duplicate issues
- [ ] `feedbacksync-test` passes
EOF
)" --estimate 90 --silent 2>&1

input.plan:
# Feedback Form + GitHub Issues Integration

## Context

Kapow has no user-facing way to submit feedback. Users need a simple form to
report bugs, request features, or send general comments. Feedback should flow
into GitHub Issues for visibility and triage. The existing beads tracker stays
for internal dev work; GitHub Issues handles user-reported feedback only.

The `internal.user_feedback` table already exists in the database with a
flexible JSONB `data` column — we build on top of it.

---

## Go GitHub Client Library

| Library | Stars | License | Pros | Cons | Fit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `google/go-github` | 11k+ | BSD-3 | Actively maintained by Google; full Issues API coverage; well-documented | REST only (v3); large dependency | Best fit — simple issue creation |
| `shurcooL/githubv4` | 1k+ | MIT | GraphQL v4; efficient for complex queries | Overkill for creating issues; less maintained | Over-engineered for this |
| `gh` CLI subprocess | N/A | N/A | No Go dependency; already installed | Fragile stdout parsing; hard to test | Fragile |
| Raw HTTP | N/A | N/A | Zero dependencies | Manual auth, pagination, error handling | Too much boilerplate |

**Recommendation**: `google/go-github` — proven, well-maintained, right level of abstraction.

---

## Architecture

```
User submits form
  → GraphQL mutation (submitFeedback)
  → Saved to internal.user_feedback table (JSONB)
  → Batch sync tool reads unsynced rows
  → Creates GitHub Issues with labels (feedback:bug, feedback:feature, feedback:general)
  → Claude triages via `gh` CLI + promotes actionable items to beads
```

---

## Implementation

### 1. Database migration (`api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`)

Add `github_issue_url` column + partial index for unsynced rows:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

### 2. GraphQL schema (`api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`)

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory { BUG  FEATURE  GENERAL }

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  email: String
  userAgent: String
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult { id: String!  success: Boolean! }

extend type Mutation {
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

### 3. Feedback service (`api/internal/feedback/service.go`)

- `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool` (follows `preference.Service` pattern)
- `Submit(ctx, input)` — validates, generates ID, marshals to JSONB, inserts
- Validation: subject required (max 200), description required (max 5000), email format if provided
- Pattern reference: `api/internal/preference/service.go`

### 4. Config flag (`api/internal/config/config.go`)

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

Document in `.env.example`.

### 5. Resolver + wiring

- Add `submitFeedback` resolver in `api/graph/resolver/` (auto-generated file after `make api-generate`)
- Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` — use user_id/email from JWT if present
- If not authenticated and `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=false`, return error
- Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` and `Config *config.Config` to `Resolver` struct
- Wire in `api/internal/router/router.go`
- Pattern reference: `api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go`

### 6. Frontend feedback form

**New files**:
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` — floating `Affix` button (bottom-right)
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` — Mantine Modal with form
- Tests alongside each component

**Form fields**: Category (Select), Subject (TextInput), Description (Textarea), Email (TextInput, only shown for anonymous users)

**Integration**: Render `<FeedbackButton />` in `App.tsx`. Uses `gql.tada` for the mutation.

Pattern reference: `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx`

### 7. GitHub sync tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Standalone Go module using `google/go-github`:

```
tools/feedbacksync/
  go.mod
  main.go       (CLI: --database-url, --github-token, --repo, --dry-run, --batch-size)
  sync.go       (query unsynced → create GH issue → update row with issue URL)
  sync_test.go
```

- Maps categories to labels: `feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`
- Auto-creates labels on first run
- Issue title: `[Feedback] {subject}`
- Issue body: Markdown with all fields + DB record ID
- Batch size default: 50

**Makefile targets**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test`

### 8. Claude Code triage workflow (`docs/specs/feedback-triage.md`)

Documented workflow using `gh` CLI:
- List open feedback issues by label
- Analyze, prioritize, add labels
- Promote actionable items to beads: `bd create --title "..." --description "From feedback: <url>"`
- Close duplicates/non-actionable with explanatory comment

---

## Key files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/internal/config/config.go` | Add `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled` field |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire `feedback.Service` + Config into resolver |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | Add `Feedback` + `Config` to Resolver struct |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | New file |
| `web/src/App.tsx` | Add `<FeedbackButton />` |
| `.env.example` | Document `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED` |
| `Makefile` | Add feedbacksync targets |

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql` | Add github_issue_url column |
| `api/internal/feedback/service.go` | Feedback service |
| `api/internal/feedback/service_test.go` | Unit tests |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | GraphQL schema |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` | Floating trigger button |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.test.tsx` | Button tests |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` | Modal with form |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx` | Modal tests |
| `tools/feedbacksync/` | GitHub sync tool (go.mod, main.go, sync.go, sync_test.go) |
| `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` | Triage workflow docs |

---

## Verification

1. `make api-generate` — codegen succeeds
2. `make test-standard` — all tests pass
3. `pnpm build && pnpm lint` — zero errors/warnings
4. Manual test: open feedback modal, submit form, verify row in `user_feedback` table
5. `cd tools/feedbacksync && go test ./...` — sync tool tests pass
6. Dry run: `go run . --dry-run` against dev DB — verify issue format
7. Live run: sync one feedback item to GitHub, verify issue created with correct labels

○ kapow-xmy.2 · GitHub Issues sync tool   [● P2 · OPEN]
Owner: Ketan Gangatirkar · Type: feature
Created: 2026-03-11 · Updated: 2026-03-11

DESCRIPTION
Thin vertical slice: a CLI tool reads unsynced feedback from the database and creates corresponding GitHub Issues with appropriate labels.

## Scope (end-to-end)
- **Tool**: `tools/feedbacksync/` — standalone Go module using `google/go-github`
- **CLI flags**: `--database-url`, `--github-token`, `--repo` (default: ketang/kapow), `--dry-run`, `--batch-size` (default: 50)
- **Sync logic**: Query rows where `github_issue_url IS NULL` → create GH issue with label (`feedback:bug`/`feedback:feature`/`feedback:general`) → update row with issue URL
- **Issue format**: Title `[Feedback] {subject}`, body with all fields + DB record ID
- **Label bootstrap**: Auto-create the three feedback labels on first run (idempotent)
- **Makefile**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test` targets
- **Tests**: Unit tests with mocked GitHub client; verify payload, label mapping, dry-run, batch limiting

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows what would be created without API calls
- [ ] Live run creates issues with correct labels and formatted body
- [ ] DB rows updated with `github_issue_url` after sync
- [ ] Re-running does not create duplicate issues
- [ ] `feedbacksync-test` passes
