input.plan:
# Feedback Form + GitHub Issues Integration

## Context

Kapow has no user-facing way to submit feedback. Users need a simple form to
report bugs, request features, or send general comments. Feedback should flow
into GitHub Issues for visibility and triage. The existing beads tracker stays
for internal dev work; GitHub Issues handles user-reported feedback only.

The `internal.user_feedback` table already exists in the database with a
flexible JSONB `data` column — we build on top of it.

---

## Go GitHub Client Library

| Library | Stars | License | Pros | Cons | Fit |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `google/go-github` | 11k+ | BSD-3 | Actively maintained by Google; full Issues API coverage; well-documented | REST only (v3); large dependency | Best fit — simple issue creation |
| `shurcooL/githubv4` | 1k+ | MIT | GraphQL v4; efficient for complex queries | Overkill for creating issues; less maintained | Over-engineered for this |
| `gh` CLI subprocess | N/A | N/A | No Go dependency; already installed | Fragile stdout parsing; hard to test | Fragile |
| Raw HTTP | N/A | N/A | Zero dependencies | Manual auth, pagination, error handling | Too much boilerplate |

**Recommendation**: `google/go-github` — proven, well-maintained, right level of abstraction.

---

## Architecture

```
User submits form
  → GraphQL mutation (submitFeedback)
  → Saved to internal.user_feedback table (JSONB)
  → Batch sync tool reads unsynced rows
  → Creates GitHub Issues with labels (feedback:bug, feedback:feature, feedback:general)
  → Claude triages via `gh` CLI + promotes actionable items to beads
```

---

## Implementation

### 1. Database migration (`api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`)

Add `github_issue_url` column + partial index for unsynced rows:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

### 2. GraphQL schema (`api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`)

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory { BUG  FEATURE  GENERAL }

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  email: String
  userAgent: String
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult { id: String!  success: Boolean! }

extend type Mutation {
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

### 3. Feedback service (`api/internal/feedback/service.go`)

- `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool` (follows `preference.Service` pattern)
- `Submit(ctx, input)` — validates, generates ID, marshals to JSONB, inserts
- Validation: subject required (max 200), description required (max 5000), email format if provided
- Pattern reference: `api/internal/preference/service.go`

### 4. Config flag (`api/internal/config/config.go`)

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

Document in `.env.example`.

### 5. Resolver + wiring

- Add `submitFeedback` resolver in `api/graph/resolver/` (auto-generated file after `make api-generate`)
- Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` — use user_id/email from JWT if present
- If not authenticated and `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=false`, return error
- Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` and `Config *config.Config` to `Resolver` struct
- Wire in `api/internal/router/router.go`
- Pattern reference: `api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go`

### 6. Frontend feedback form

**New files**:
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` — floating `Affix` button (bottom-right)
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` — Mantine Modal with form
- Tests alongside each component

**Form fields**: Category (Select), Subject (TextInput), Description (Textarea), Email (TextInput, only shown for anonymous users)

**Integration**: Render `<FeedbackButton />` in `App.tsx`. Uses `gql.tada` for the mutation.

Pattern reference: `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx`

### 7. GitHub sync tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Standalone Go module using `google/go-github`:

```
tools/feedbacksync/
  go.mod
  main.go       (CLI: --database-url, --github-token, --repo, --dry-run, --batch-size)
  sync.go       (query unsynced → create GH issue → update row with issue URL)
  sync_test.go
```

- Maps categories to labels: `feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`
- Auto-creates labels on first run
- Issue title: `[Feedback] {subject}`
- Issue body: Markdown with all fields + DB record ID
- Batch size default: 50

**Makefile targets**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test`

### 8. Claude Code triage workflow (`docs/specs/feedback-triage.md`)

Documented workflow using `gh` CLI:
- List open feedback issues by label
- Analyze, prioritize, add labels
- Promote actionable items to beads: `bd create --title "..." --description "From feedback: <url>"`
- Close duplicates/non-actionable with explanatory comment

---

## Key files to modify

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/internal/config/config.go` | Add `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled` field |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire `feedback.Service` + Config into resolver |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | Add `Feedback` + `Config` to Resolver struct |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | New file |
| `web/src/App.tsx` | Add `<FeedbackButton />` |
| `.env.example` | Document `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED` |
| `Makefile` | Add feedbacksync targets |

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql` | Add github_issue_url column |
| `api/internal/feedback/service.go` | Feedback service |
| `api/internal/feedback/service_test.go` | Unit tests |
| `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql` | GraphQL schema |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` | Floating trigger button |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.test.tsx` | Button tests |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` | Modal with form |
| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx` | Modal tests |
| `tools/feedbacksync/` | GitHub sync tool (go.mod, main.go, sync.go, sync_test.go) |
| `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` | Triage workflow docs |

---

## Verification

1. `make api-generate` — codegen succeeds
2. `make test-standard` — all tests pass
3. `pnpm build && pnpm lint` — zero errors/warnings
4. Manual test: open feedback modal, submit form, verify row in `user_feedback` table
5. `cd tools/feedbacksync && go test ./...` — sync tool tests pass
6. Dry run: `go run . --dry-run` against dev DB — verify issue format
7. Live run: sync one feedback item to GitHub, verify issue created with correct labels

|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `google/go-github` | 11k+ | BSD-3 | Actively maintained by Google; full Issues API coverage; well-documented | REST only (v3); large dependency | Best fit — simple issue creation |
| `shurcooL/githubv4` | 1k+ | MIT | GraphQL v4; efficient for complex queries | Overkill for creating issues; less maintained | Over-engineered for this |
| `gh` CLI subprocess | N/A | N/A | No Go dependency; already installed | Fragile stdout parsing; hard to test | Fragile |
| Raw HTTP | N/A | N/A | Zero dependencies | Manual auth, pagination, error handling | Too much boilerplate |

**Recommendation**: `google/go-github` — proven, well-maintained, right level of abstraction.

---

## Architecture

```
User submits form
  → GraphQL mutation (submitFeedback)
  → Saved to internal.user_feedback table (JSONB)
  → Batch sync tool reads unsynced rows
  → Creates GitHub Issues with labels (feedback:bug, feedback:feature, feedback:general)
  → Claude triages via `gh` CLI + promotes actionable items to beads
```

---

## Implementation

### 1. Database migration (`api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`)

Add `github_issue_url` column + partial index for unsynced rows:

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS internal.user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

### 2. GraphQL schema (`api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`)

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory { BUG  FEATURE  GENERAL }

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  email: String
  userAgent: String
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult { id: String!  success: Boolean! }

extend type Mutation {
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

### 3. Feedback service (`api/internal/feedback/service.go`)

- `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool` (follows `preference.Service` pattern)
- `Submit(ctx, input)` — validates, generates ID, marshals to JSONB, inserts
- Validation: subject required (max 200), description required (max 5000), email format if provided
- Pattern reference: `api/internal/preference/service.go`

### 4. Config flag (`api/internal/config/config.go`)

```go
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

Document in `.env.example`.

### 5. Resolver + wiring

- Add `submitFeedback` resolver in `api/graph/resolver/` (auto-generated file after `make api-generate`)
- Read `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` — use user_id/email from JWT if present
- If not authenticated and `FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=false`, return error
- Add `Feedback *feedback.Service` and `Config *config.Config` to `Resolver` struct
- Wire in `api/internal/router/router.go`
- Pattern reference: `api/graph/resolver/preferences.resolvers.go`

### 6. Frontend feedback form

**New files**:
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx` — floating `Affix` button (bottom-right)
- `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx` — Mantine Modal with form
- Tests alongside each component

**Form fields**: Category (Select), Subject (TextInput), Description (Textarea), Email (TextInput, only shown for anonymous users)

**Integration**: Render `<FeedbackButton />` in `App.tsx`. Uses `gql.tada` for the mutation.

Pattern reference: `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx`

### 7. GitHub sync tool (`tools/feedbacksync/`)

Standalone Go module using `google/go-github`:

```
tools/feedbacksync/
  go.mod
  main.go       (CLI: --database-url, --github-token, --repo, --dry-run, --batch-size)
  sync.go       (query unsynced → create GH issue → update row with issue URL)
  sync_test.go
```

- Maps categories to labels: `feedback:bug`, `feedback:feature`, `feedback:general`
- Auto-creates labels on first run
- Issue title: `[Feedback] {subject}`
- Issue body: Markdown with all fields + DB record ID
- Batch size default: 50

**Makefile targets**: `feedbacksync-build`, `feedbacksync-test`

### 8. Claude Code triage workflow (`docs/specs/feedback-triage.md`)

Documented workflow using `gh` CLI:
- List open feedback issues by label
- Analyze, prioritize, add labels
- Promote actionable items to beads: `bd create --title "..." --description "From feedback: <url>"`
- Close duplicates/non-actionable with explanatory comment

---

## Key files to modify

| `api/internal/config/config.go` | Add `FeedbackAnonymousEnabled` field |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire `feedback.Service` + Config into resolver |

| `web/src/App.tsx` | Add `<FeedbackButton />` |

## New files

| `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx` | Modal tests |

| `docs/specs/feedback-triage.md` | Triage workflow docs |

---

## Verification

1. `make api-generate` — codegen succeeds
2. `make test-standard` — all tests pass
3. `pnpm build && pnpm lint` — zero errors/warnings
4. Manual test: open feedback modal, submit form, verify row in `user_feedback` table
5. `cd tools/feedbacksync && go test ./...` — sync tool tests pass
6. Dry run: `go run . --dry-run` against dev DB — verify issue format
7. Live run: sync one feedback item to GitHub, verify issue created with correct labels

   176→// authOrIPKeyFunc keys by user subject if authenticated, otherwise by client IP.
   177→func authOrIPKeyFunc(r *http.Request) (string, error) {
   178→	if claims := auth.GetClaims(r.Context()); claims != nil {
   179→		return "user:" + claims.Subject, nil
   180→	}
   181→	return "ip:" + r.RemoteAddr, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→// authRateLimitOverride returns middleware that raises the httprate request limit
   185→// for authenticated users by setting WithRequestLimit in the request context.
   186→func authRateLimitOverride(authLimit int) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
   187→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
   188→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   189→			if auth.GetClaims(r.Context()) != nil {
   190→				r = r.WithContext(httprate.WithRequestLimit(r.Context(), authLimit))
   191→			}
   192→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
   193→		})
   194→	}
   195→}

    69→// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    70→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    71→	cfg := &Config{}
    72→	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    73→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    74→	}

    77→
    78→// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    79→func (c *Config) Addr() string {

    82→
    83→// LevelTrace is a custom log level below DEBUG for very detailed diagnostic
    84→// output. Usage: logger.Log(ctx, config.LevelTrace, "msg", "key", val)
    85→const LevelTrace = slog.Level(-8)
    86→
    87→// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    88→func ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    89→	switch s {
    90→	case "trace":
    91→		return LevelTrace
    92→	case "debug":
    93→		return slog.LevelDebug
    94→	case "warn":
    95→		return slog.LevelWarn
    96→	case "error":
    97→		return slog.LevelError
    98→	default:
    99→		return slog.LevelInfo
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// LogHandler returns an slog.Handler configured per LOG_LEVEL and LOG_FORMAT.
   104→// The handler uses a dynamic LevelVar so the log level can be changed at
   105→// runtime via LogLevelVar().Set() without recreating the handler. Output is
   106→// routed through a SwappableWriter and wrapped with a CategoryHandler for
   107→// per-category level overrides.
   108→func (c *Config) LogHandler() slog.Handler {
   109→	c.logLevelVar.Set(ParseLogLevel(c.LogLevel))
   110→	c.swappableWriter = NewSwappableWriter()
   111→
   112→	opts := &slog.HandlerOptions{
   113→		Level:     &c.logLevelVar,
   114→		AddSource: c.Env == "development",
   115→		ReplaceAttr: func(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {
   116→			if a.Key == slog.LevelKey {
   117→				if level, ok := a.Value.Any().(slog.Level); ok && level == LevelTrace {
   118→					a.Value = slog.StringValue("TRACE")
   119→				}
   120→			}
   121→			return a
   122→		},
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	var inner slog.Handler
   126→	if c.LogFormat == "text" {
   127→		inner = slog.NewTextHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   128→	} else {
   129→		inner = slog.NewJSONHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	c.categoryHandler = NewCategoryHandler(inner, &c.logLevelVar)
   133→	return c.categoryHandler
   134→}
   135→
   136→// LogLevelVar returns the dynamic log level variable. Callers can change
   137→// the active log level at runtime by calling Set() on the returned LevelVar.
   138→func (c *Config) LogLevelVar() *slog.LevelVar {
   139→	return &c.logLevelVar
   140→}
   141→
   142→// LogWriter returns the swappable writer used for log output.
   143→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   144→func (c *Config) LogWriter() *SwappableWriter {
   145→	return c.swappableWriter
   146→}
   147→
   148→// LogCategoryHandler returns the category handler for per-category level overrides.
   149→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   150→func (c *Config) LogCategoryHandler() *CategoryHandler {
   151→	return c.categoryHandler
   152→}
   153→
   154→// HTTPDebugEnabled reports whether any HTTP debug logging is configured.
   155→func (c *Config) HTTPDebugEnabled() bool {
   156→	return c.HTTPDebugRequestHeaders != "none" || c.HTTPDebugResponseMode != "none"
   157→}
   158→
   159→// ParsedSelectedHeaders splits the comma-separated HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders
   160→// into a trimmed slice of header names.
   161→func (c *Config) ParsedSelectedHeaders() []string {
   162→	raw := strings.Split(c.HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders, ",")
   163→	out := make([]string, 0, len(raw))
   164→	for _, h := range raw {
   165→		h = strings.TrimSpace(h)
   166→		if h != "" {
   167→			out = append(out, h)
   168→		}
   169→	}
   170→	return out
   171→}
   172→

1→type Preferences {
     2→  homeLatitude: Float
     3→  homeLongitude: Float
     4→  homeState: String
     5→}
     6→
     7→input UpdatePreferencesInput {
     8→  homeLatitude: Float
     9→  homeLongitude: Float
    10→  homeState: String
    11→}
    12→
    13→extend type Query {
    14→  """
    15→  Returns the current user's preferences. Requires authentication.
    16→  Returns default (empty) preferences if none have been saved.
    17→  """
    18→  preferences: Preferences!
    19→}
    20→
    21→type Mutation {
    22→  """
    23→  Updates the current user's preferences. Requires authentication.
    24→  Replaces the entire preferences object with the provided input.
    25→  """
    26→  updatePreferences(input: UpdatePreferencesInput!): Preferences!
    27→}
    28→

1→import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { Modal, Stack, Select, Alert, Group, Text } from '@mantine/core'
     3→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     4→import { LocationInput, type UserLocationValue } from '@/components/search/LocationInput'
     5→import { US_STATES } from '@/components/search/constants'
     6→import { usePreferencesStore } from '@/stores/preferencesStore'
     7→import { MapPin } from 'lucide-react'
     8→
     9→interface PreferencesModalProps {
    10→  opened: boolean
    11→  onClose: () => void
    12→}
    13→
    14→type FeedbackState = { type: 'success' | 'error'; message: string } | null
    15→
    16→export function PreferencesModal({ opened, onClose }: PreferencesModalProps) {
    17→  const homeLatitude = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeLatitude)
    18→  const homeLongitude = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeLongitude)
    19→  const homeState = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeState)
    20→  const loaded = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.loaded)
    21→  const load = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.load)
    22→  const update = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.update)
    23→
    24→  const [location, setLocation] = useState<UserLocationValue | undefined>(undefined)
    25→  const [state, setState] = useState<string | null>(null)
    26→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false)
    27→  const [feedback, setFeedback] = useState<FeedbackState>(null)
    28→
    29→  // Load preferences when modal opens
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    if (opened) {
    32→      void load()
    33→    }
    34→  }, [opened, load])
    35→
    36→  // Sync store values into local state when loaded
    37→  useEffect(() => {
    38→    if (!loaded) return
    39→    if (homeLatitude != null && homeLongitude != null) {
    40→      setLocation({
    41→        latitude: homeLatitude,
    42→        longitude: homeLongitude,
    43→        state: homeState ?? undefined,
    44→      })
    45→    } else {
    46→      setLocation(undefined)
    47→    }
    48→    setState(homeState)
    49→  }, [loaded, homeLatitude, homeLongitude, homeState])
    50→
    51→  // Clear feedback when modal closes
    52→  useEffect(() => {
    53→    if (!opened) setFeedback(null)
    54→  }, [opened])
    55→
    56→  const handleSave = useCallback(async () => {
    57→    setSaving(true)
    58→    setFeedback(null)
    59→    try {
    60→      await update({
    61→        homeLatitude: location?.latitude ?? null,
    62→        homeLongitude: location?.longitude ?? null,
    63→        homeState: state ?? location?.state ?? null,
    64→      })
    65→      setFeedback({ type: 'success', message: 'Preferences saved.' })
    66→    } catch {
    67→      setFeedback({ type: 'error', message: 'Failed to save preferences.' })
    68→    } finally {
    69→      setSaving(false)
    70→    }
    71→  }, [location, state, update])
    72→
    73→  const handleClear = useCallback(async () => {
    74→    setSaving(true)
    75→    setFeedback(null)
    76→    try {
    77→      await update({
    78→        homeLatitude: null,
    79→        homeLongitude: null,
    80→        homeState: null,
    81→      })
    82→      setLocation(undefined)
    83→      setState(null)
    84→      setFeedback({ type: 'success', message: 'Preferences cleared.' })
    85→    } catch {
    86→      setFeedback({ type: 'error', message: 'Failed to clear preferences.' })
    87→    } finally {
    88→      setSaving(false)
    89→    }
    90→  }, [update])
    91→
    92→  const handleLocationChange = useCallback((loc: UserLocationValue | undefined) => {
    93→    setLocation(loc)
    94→    // Auto-set state from location if available and state not manually set
    95→    if (loc?.state && !state) {
    96→      setState(loc.state)
    97→    }
    98→    setFeedback(null)
    99→  }, [state])
   100→
   101→  const hasPreferences = location != null || state != null
   102→
   103→  return (
   104→    <Modal opened={opened} onClose={onClose} title="Preferences" size="sm">
   105→      <Stack gap="md">
   106→        <Stack gap="xs">
   107→          <Text size="sm" fw={500}>
   108→            Home location
   109→          </Text>
   110→          <LocationInput value={location} onChange={handleLocationChange} />
   111→        </Stack>
   112→
   113→        <Select
   114→          label="Home state"
   115→          placeholder="Select a state"
   116→          data={US_STATES}
   117→          value={state}
   118→          onChange={setState}
   119→          searchable
   120→          clearable
   121→        />
   122→
   123→        {feedback && (
   124→          <Alert
   125→            color={feedback.type === 'success' ? 'green' : 'red'}
   126→            variant="light"
   127→          >
   128→            {feedback.message}
   129→          </Alert>
   130→        )}
   131→
   132→        <Group justify="space-between">
   133→          <Button
   134→            variant="light"
   135→            size="xs"
   136→            onClick={() => void handleClear()}
   137→            disabled={saving || !hasPreferences}
   138→          >
   139→            Clear all
   140→          </Button>
   141→          <Group gap="xs">
   142→            <Button variant="outline" size="xs" onClick={onClose}>
   143→              Cancel
   144→            </Button>
   145→            <Button
   146→              variant="accent"
   147→              size="xs"
   148→              onClick={() => void handleSave()}
   149→              disabled={saving}
   150→              leftSection={<MapPin size={14} />}
   151→            >
   152→              {saving ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}
   153→            </Button>
   154→          </Group>
   155→        </Group>
   156→      </Stack>
   157→    </Modal>
   158→  )
   159→}
   160→

1→/**
     2→ * Strict GraphQL utilities — re-exports urql hooks with types that reject raw strings.
     3→ *
     4→ * Import useQuery/useMutation from here instead of 'urql'. Raw string queries
     5→ * bypass gql.tada's compile-time schema validation, which has caused production
     6→ * bugs (e.g. querying fields that don't exist in the schema).
     7→ *
     8→ * These wrappers require a parsed DocumentNode (has 'kind' and 'definitions'),
     9→ * which gql.tada's graphql() returns but raw strings do not satisfy.
    10→ *
    11→ * Usage:
    12→ *   import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
    13→ *   import { useQuery, useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'
    14→ *
    15→ *   const MyQuery = graphql(`query MyQuery { ... }`)
    16→ *   const [result] = useQuery({ query: MyQuery })
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  useQuery as _useQuery,
    21→  useMutation as _useMutation,
    22→  type UseQueryResponse,
    23→  type UseMutationResponse,
    24→  type AnyVariables,
    25→  type OperationContext,
    26→  type RequestPolicy,
    27→} from 'urql'
    28→
    29→// A parsed GraphQL document has { kind: 'Document', definitions: [...] }.
    30→// gql.tada's graphql() returns this at runtime. Raw strings don't have these
    31→// properties, so TypeScript rejects them.
    32→interface ParsedDocument {
    33→  readonly kind: string
    34→  readonly definitions: readonly unknown[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→interface StrictQueryArgs<Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables> {
    38→  query: ParsedDocument
    39→  variables?: Variables
    40→  requestPolicy?: RequestPolicy
    41→  context?: Partial<OperationContext>
    42→  pause?: boolean
    43→}
    44→
    45→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    46→export function useQuery<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    47→  args: StrictQueryArgs<Variables>,
    48→): UseQueryResponse<Data, Variables> {
    49→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    50→  return _useQuery(args as any)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    54→export function useMutation<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    55→  query: ParsedDocument,
    56→): UseMutationResponse<Data, Variables> {
    57→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    58→  return _useMutation(query as any)
    59→}
    60→

1→# ─── Kapow API — Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values.

     4→
     5→# ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

     8→

    10→# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)

    13→# For `make dev-db-up` (standalone dev database on port 5433):

    15→
    16→# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17→# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
    18→JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    19→
    20→# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
    21→# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
    22→
    23→# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)

    25→
    26→# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)

    28→
    29→# ── Supabase shortcut ──

    37→
    38→# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────

    40→# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
    41→# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co

    46→# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
    47→
    48→# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    49→LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
    50→LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)
    51→
    52→# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53→# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
    54→# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
    55→# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"

    57→# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)

    76→MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp

    86→# OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=kapow-api   (overridden by code — set in Config)
    87→# See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/
    88→
    89→# ─── Shutdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90→SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT=15s
    91→
    92→# ─── Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→MAX_EXPORT_ROWS=500                # Maximum rows in CSV export (default: 500)
    94→
    95→# ─── Rate Limiting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96→RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true            # Master switch for all rate limits
    97→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL=60              # /graphql requests per minute (anonymous)
    98→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH=120        # /graphql requests per minute (authenticated)
    99→RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT=10               # /api/export/csv requests per minute (per user)
   100→RATE_LIMIT_MCP=120                 # /mcp/* requests per minute (per user)
   101→

   103→# Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to access /private/* routes
   104→PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS=127.0.0.0/8,::1/128

# ─── Kapow API — Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────
# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values.
# NEVER commit .env to version control.

# ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HOST=0.0.0.0
PORT=8081

# ─── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)
# Required — server will not start without this.
DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
# For `make dev-db-up` (standalone dev database on port 5433):
# DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable

# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret

# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----

# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)
# JWT_ISSUER=https://auth.example.com

# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)
# JWT_AUDIENCE=kapow-api

# ── Supabase shortcut ──
# Use the JWT secret from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → JWT Secret:
# JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
# JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
# JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated

# Future: OIDC discovery URL (not yet active)
# OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL=https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration

# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────
# Project URL and anon key from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API.
# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# Vite exposes these to the browser (VITE_ prefix required)
# VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...

# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)

# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"
# HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS=Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent
# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)
# HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE=true
# Response logging: none (off) | headers | full (headers + body)
# HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE=none
# Max response body bytes to capture when mode is "full"
# HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES=4096

# ─── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ENV=development         # development | staging | production

# ─── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Comma-separated allowed origins (empty = no CORS headers set)
CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080

# ─── GraphQL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true     # Enable Playground UI (dev only — disable in prod)
GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION=true  # Enable schema introspection

# ─── MCP (Model Context Protocol) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp
# MCP requires a valid JWT — same tokens used for /graphql work for /mcp.
# OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata is served at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource

# ─── OpenTelemetry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Set to true to enable OTLP HTTP export of traces and metrics.
OTEL_ENABLED=false
# Standard OTel SDK env vars (only used when OTEL_ENABLED=true):
# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com:4318
# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS=x-api-key=YOUR_KEY
# OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=kapow-api   (overridden by code — set in Config)
# See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/

# ─── Shutdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT=15s

# ─── Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MAX_EXPORT_ROWS=500                # Maximum rows in CSV export (default: 500)

# ─── Rate Limiting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true            # Master switch for all rate limits
RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL=60              # /graphql requests per minute (anonymous)
RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH=120        # /graphql requests per minute (authenticated)
RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT=10               # /api/export/csv requests per minute (per user)
RATE_LIMIT_MCP=120                 # /mcp/* requests per minute (per user)

# ─── Private Endpoint Access ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to access /private/* routes
PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS=127.0.0.0/8,::1/128

schema:
  - graph/schema/*.graphql

exec:
  filename: graph/generated/generated.go
  package: generated

model:
  filename: graph/model/models_gen.go
  package: model

resolver:
  layout: follow-schema
  dir: graph/resolver
  package: resolver
  filename_template: "{name}.resolvers.go"

autobind: []

models:
  FilterValue:
    model: github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/scalar.FilterValue
  Theme:
    model: github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search.Theme
  FieldVisibility:
    model: github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search.FieldVisibility
  FieldSearchability:
    model: github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search.FieldSearchability
  SearchFieldName:
    model: github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search.SearchFieldName

input.prompt:
Implement the backend portion of the feedback feature for the Kapow project. Here's exactly what to build:

## 1. Migration: `api/migrations/00010_feedback_github_sync.sql`

```sql
-- +goose Up
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback ADD COLUMN github_issue_url TEXT;
CREATE INDEX user_feedback_unsynced_idx ON internal.user_feedback (created)
  WHERE github_issue_url IS NULL;

-- +goose Down
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS user_feedback_unsynced_idx;
ALTER TABLE internal.user_feedback DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS github_issue_url;
```

## 2. GraphQL Schema: `api/graph/schema/feedback.graphql`

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory {
  BUG
  FEATURE
  GENERAL
}

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  """Optional email for anonymous submissions."""
  email: String
  """Browser user-agent string (auto-populated by frontend)."""
  userAgent: String
  """Page URL where feedback was submitted."""
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult {
  id: String!
  success: Boolean!
}

extend type Mutation {
  """
  Submit user feedback. Works for both authenticated and anonymous users.
  If authenticated, user_id and email are taken from the JWT.
  """
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

## 3. Feedback Service: `api/internal/feedback/service.go`

Follow the pattern from `api/internal/preference/service.go`:
- `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool`
- `NewService(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service`
- `Submit(ctx context.Context, input SubmitInput) (string, error)` method

```go
// SubmitInput holds the data for a feedback submission.
type SubmitInput struct {
    Category  string
    Subject   string
    Description string
    Email     string // optional, for anonymous users
    UserAgent string // optional
    PageURL   string // optional
    UserID    string // from JWT if authenticated
    UserEmail string // from JWT if authenticated
}
```

The Submit method should:
- Validate: subject required and max 200 chars, description required and max 5000 chars
- Generate a UUID for the ID using `gen_random_uuid()` via SQL (or use `crypto/rand` to generate one in Go)
- Marshal category, subject, description, userAgent, pageUrl into a JSONB map for the `data` column
- Use user_id and user_email from the input (caller provides from JWT claims)
- If no user_id, use the email from input
- Return the generated ID
- Error format: `fmt.Errorf("feedback: context: %w", err)`

SQL insert: `INSERT INTO internal.user_feedback (id, user_id, user_email, data) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4) RETURNING id`

For generating the ID, use a UUID v4 approach. You can use `crypto/rand` to generate it since the table expects VARCHAR(62).

## 4. Unit Tests: `api/internal/feedback/service_test.go`

Test the validation logic:
- Empty subject returns error
- Subject > 200 chars returns error
- Empty description returns error
- Description > 5000 chars returns error
- Valid input passes validation (you can test the Validate method separately, or test Submit with a mock — but validation should be testable without a DB)

Extract a `validate` method from Submit that can be unit tested independently.

## 5. Config: Add to `api/internal/config/config.go`

Add this field to the Config struct, after the rate limiting section:

```go
// Feedback
FeedbackAnonymousEnabled bool `env:"FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
```

## 6. Run `make api-generate` after creating the schema

After writing the feedback.graphql file, run `make api-generate` from the project root. This will generate:
- `api/graph/generated/generated.go` (updated)
- `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` (updated with FeedbackCategory, SubmitFeedbackInput, FeedbackResult)
- `api/graph/resolver/feedback.resolvers.go` (new file with stub resolver)

## 7. Implement the resolver in `api/graph/resolver/feedback.resolvers.go`

After generation, the file will have a stub `SubmitFeedback` method. Implement it:
- Get claims from context: `auth.GetClaims(ctx)`
- If no claims and anonymous feedback disabled (check `r.Config.FeedbackAnonymousEnabled`), return error
- Build `feedback.SubmitInput` from the model input + claims
- Call `r.Feedback.Submit(ctx, input)`
- Return `&model.FeedbackResult{ID: id, Success: true}`

## 8. Wire into resolver and router

**`api/graph/resolver/resolver.go`** — add to the Resolver struct:
```go
Feedback *feedback.Service
Config   *config.Config
```

Import the feedback and config packages.

**`api/internal/router/router.go`** — update the resolver creation on line 78:
```go
Resolvers: &resolver.Resolver{
    DB:         deps.DB,
    Search:     deps.Search,
    Preference: deps.PreferenceService,
    Feedback:   feedbackService,
    Config:     deps.Config,
},
```

Create `feedbackService` before building the resolver:
```go
feedbackService := feedback.NewService(deps.DB)
```

Import the feedback package.

## 9. Update `.env.example`

Add after the rate limiting section:
```
# ─── Feedback ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Allow anonymous (unauthenticated) feedback submissions
FEEDBACK_ANONYMOUS_ENABLED=true
```

## Important conventions
- Error format: `fmt.Errorf("package: context: %w", err)`
- Always use parameterized SQL ($1, $2...)
- Context as first parameter
- Structured logging with slog
- No init() globals

Working directory is `/home/ketan/project/kapow`. Make all file paths absolute.

1→package resolver
     2→
     3→// This file will not be regenerated automatically.
     4→//
     5→// It serves as dependency injection for your app, add any dependencies you require here.
     6→
     7→import (
     8→	"regexp"
     9→
    10→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/db"
    11→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/preference"
    12→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    13→)
    14→
    15→var zipRegexp = regexp.MustCompile(`^\d{5}$`)
    16→
    17→// Resolver is the root resolver, holding injected application dependencies.
    18→type Resolver struct {
    19→	DB         db.Querier
    20→	Search     *search.Registry
    21→	Preference *preference.Service
    22→}
    23→

1→package router
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"io/fs"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"net"
     7→	"net/http"
     8→	"sync/atomic"
     9→	"time"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler"
    12→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler/extension"
    13→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/handler/transport"
    14→	"github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql/playground"
    15→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    16→	chimiddleware "github.com/go-chi/chi/v5/middleware"
    17→	"github.com/go-chi/httprate"
    18→	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
    19→
    20→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/generated"
    21→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/graph/resolver"
    22→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/auth"
    23→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/config"
    24→	kapowhandler "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/handler"
    25→	kapowmcp "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/mcp"
    26→	kapowmiddleware "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/middleware"
    27→	kapowotel "github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/otel"
    28→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/preference"
    29→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/search"
    30→	"github.com/ketang/kapow/api/internal/user"
    31→)
    32→
    33→// Deps holds the dependencies needed to construct the router.
    34→type Deps struct {
    35→	Config            *config.Config
    36→	DB                *pgxpool.Pool
    37→	Validator         auth.Validator
    38→	Search            *search.Registry
    39→	UserService       *user.Service
    40→	PreferenceService *preference.Service
    41→
    42→	// HTTPDetailConfig holds the hot-reloadable HTTP debug logging config.
    43→	// A future admin endpoint can call .Store() to change settings at runtime.
    44→	// If nil, New() initialises it from Config.
    45→	HTTPDetailConfig *atomic.Pointer[kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetailConfig]
    46→
    47→	// PrivateCIDRs restricts /private/* routes to these IP ranges.
    48→	PrivateCIDRs []*net.IPNet
    49→
    50→	// UI holds the embedded SPA filesystem. When non-nil, unmatched routes
    51→	// serve the SPA (static files + index.html fallback).
    52→	UI fs.FS
    53→}
    54→
    55→// New constructs the chi router with all middleware, routes, and handlers.
    56→func New(deps Deps) http.Handler {
    57→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    58→
    59→	// Initialise hot-reloadable HTTP detail config if not provided.
    60→	if deps.HTTPDetailConfig == nil {
    61→		deps.HTTPDetailConfig = &atomic.Pointer[kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetailConfig]{}
    62→		deps.HTTPDetailConfig.Store(kapowmiddleware.NewHTTPDetailConfig(deps.Config))
    63→	}
    64→
    65→	// Global middleware (applied to every request)
    66→	if deps.Config.OTelEnabled {
    67→		r.Use(kapowotel.Middleware("kapow-api"))
    68→	}
    69→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.RequestID)
    70→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Logging)
    71→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Recovery)
    72→	r.Use(kapowmiddleware.HTTPDetail(deps.HTTPDetailConfig))
    73→	r.Use(chimiddleware.RealIP)
    74→	r.Use(chimiddleware.StripSlashes)
    75→
    76→	// Build gqlgen server
    77→	gqlSrv := handler.New(generated.NewExecutableSchema(generated.Config{
    78→		Resolvers: &resolver.Resolver{DB: deps.DB, Search: deps.Search, Preference: deps.PreferenceService},
    79→	}))
    80→	gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
    81→	gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
    82→	if deps.Config.GraphQLIntrospection {
    83→		gqlSrv.Use(extension.Introspection{})
    84→	}
    85→
    86→	// GraphQL endpoint — optional auth (anonymous allowed, user provisioned if token present)
    87→	r.Route("/graphql", func(r chi.Router) {
    88→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
    89→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.EnsureUser(deps.UserService))
    90→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
    91→			r.Use(authRateLimitOverride(deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQLAuth))
    92→			r.Use(httprate.Limit(
    93→				deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQL,
    94→				time.Minute,
    95→				httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
    96→			))
    97→		}
    98→		r.Post("/", gqlSrv.ServeHTTP)
    99→		r.Get("/", gqlSrv.ServeHTTP)
   100→	})
   101→
   102→	// Dev-only mock auth endpoint — issues JWTs without Supabase
   103→	if deps.Config.Env == "development" {
   104→		r.Post("/dev/auth/login", kapowhandler.DevLogin(deps.Config.JWTSecret))
   105→		slog.Info("router: dev auth endpoint enabled at /dev/auth/login")
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	// GraphQL Playground (dev only)
   109→	if deps.Config.GraphQLPlayground {
   110→		r.Get("/playground", playground.Handler("Kapow API", "/graphql"))
   111→		slog.Info("router: GraphQL Playground enabled at /playground")
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	// CSV export endpoint — optional auth + rate limited
   115→	r.Route("/api/export", func(r chi.Router) {
   116→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
   117→		r.Use(kapowmiddleware.EnsureUser(deps.UserService))
   118→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
   119→			r.Use(httprate.Limit(
   120→				deps.Config.RateLimitExport,
   121→				time.Minute,
   122→				httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
   123→			))
   124→		}
   125→		r.Get("/csv", kapowhandler.ExportCSV(deps.DB, deps.Search, deps.Config.MaxExportRows))
   126→	})
   127→
   128→	// Private (internal) routes — IP allowlisted
   129→	r.Route("/private", func(r chi.Router) {
   130→		if len(deps.PrivateCIDRs) > 0 {
   131→			r.Use(kapowmiddleware.IPAllowlist(deps.PrivateCIDRs))
   132→		}
   133→		r.Get("/healthcheck", kapowhandler.Health(deps.DB))
   134→		r.Get("/log-config", kapowhandler.LogConfig(deps.Config))
   135→		r.Put("/log-config", kapowhandler.LogConfig(deps.Config))
   136→	})
   137→
   138→	// MCP (Model Context Protocol) endpoint — AI client integration
   139→	// Protected with mandatory Auth middleware; unauthenticated requests
   140→	// receive 401 with WWW-Authenticate header per MCP OAuth 2.1 spec.
   141→	if deps.Config.MCPEnabled {
   142→		r.Route("/mcp", func(r chi.Router) {
   143→			r.Use(kapowmiddleware.Auth(deps.Validator))
   144→			if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
   145→				r.Use(httprate.Limit(
   146→					deps.Config.RateLimitMCP,
   147→					time.Minute,
   148→					httprate.WithKeyFuncs(authOrIPKeyFunc),
   149→				))
   150→			}
   151→			r.Handle("/*", kapowmcp.NewHandler(deps.DB, deps.Search))
   152→		})
   153→		slog.Info("router: MCP endpoint enabled at /mcp")
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	// OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata (RFC 9728)
   157→	r.Get("/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource", kapowhandler.ProtectedResourceMetadata(deps.Config))
   158→
   159→	// Embedded SPA — serve static files and fall back to index.html
   160→	if deps.UI != nil {
   161→		r.NotFound(kapowhandler.SPAHandler(deps.UI).ServeHTTP)
   162→		slog.Info("router: embedded SPA enabled")
   163→	}
   164→
   165→	slog.Info("router: initialized",
   166→		"graphql_introspection", deps.Config.GraphQLIntrospection,
   167→		"graphql_playground", deps.Config.GraphQLPlayground,
   168→		"mcp_enabled", deps.Config.MCPEnabled,
   169→		"rate_limit_enabled", deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled,
   170→		"embedded_ui", deps.UI != nil,
   171→	)
   172→
   173→	return r
   174→}
   175→
   176→// authOrIPKeyFunc keys by user subject if authenticated, otherwise by client IP.
   177→func authOrIPKeyFunc(r *http.Request) (string, error) {
   178→	if claims := auth.GetClaims(r.Context()); claims != nil {
   179→		return "user:" + claims.Subject, nil
   180→	}
   181→	return "ip:" + r.RemoteAddr, nil
   182→}
   183→
   184→// authRateLimitOverride returns middleware that raises the httprate request limit
   185→// for authenticated users by setting WithRequestLimit in the request context.
   186→func authRateLimitOverride(authLimit int) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
   187→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
   188→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   189→			if auth.GetClaims(r.Context()) != nil {
   190→				r = r.WithContext(httprate.WithRequestLimit(r.Context(), authLimit))
   191→			}
   192→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
   193→		})
   194→	}
   195→}
   196→

1→package config
     2→
     3→import (
     4→	"fmt"
     5→	"log/slog"
     6→	"strings"
     7→	"time"
     8→
     9→	"github.com/caarlos0/env/v11"
    10→)
    11→
    12→// Config holds all application configuration loaded from environment variables.
    13→type Config struct {
    14→	Host string `env:"HOST" envDefault:"0.0.0.0"`
    15→	Port int    `env:"PORT" envDefault:"8081"`
    16→
    17→	DatabaseURL string `env:"DATABASE_URL,required"`
    18→
    19→	JWTSecret    string `env:"JWT_SECRET"` //nolint:gosec // G117: config field name, not a hardcoded secret
    20→	JWTPublicKey string `env:"JWT_PUBLIC_KEY"`
    21→	JWTIssuer    string `env:"JWT_ISSUER"`
    22→	JWTAudience  string `env:"JWT_AUDIENCE"`
    23→
    24→	OIDCDiscoveryURL string `env:"OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL"`
    25→
    26→	LogLevel  string `env:"LOG_LEVEL" envDefault:"info"`
    27→	LogFormat string `env:"LOG_FORMAT" envDefault:"json"`
    28→
    29→	Env string `env:"ENV" envDefault:"development"`
    30→
    31→	CORSOrigins string `env:"CORS_ORIGINS"`
    32→
    33→	GraphQLPlayground    bool `env:"GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND" envDefault:"false"`
    34→	GraphQLIntrospection bool `env:"GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION" envDefault:"true"`
    35→
    36→	MCPEnabled  bool `env:"MCP_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    37→	OTelEnabled bool `env:"OTEL_ENABLED" envDefault:"false"`
    38→
    39→	ShutdownTimeout time.Duration `env:"SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT" envDefault:"15s"`
    40→
    41→	MaxExportRows int `env:"MAX_EXPORT_ROWS" envDefault:"500"`
    42→
    43→	// Rate limiting
    44→	RateLimitEnabled     bool   `env:"RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED" envDefault:"true"`
    45→	RateLimitGraphQL     int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL" envDefault:"60"`
    46→	RateLimitGraphQLAuth int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH" envDefault:"120"`
    47→	RateLimitExport      int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT" envDefault:"10"`
    48→	RateLimitMCP         int    `env:"RATE_LIMIT_MCP" envDefault:"120"`
    49→	PrivateAllowlistCIDRs string `env:"PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS" envDefault:"127.0.0.0/8,::1/128"`
    50→
    51→	// HTTP debug logging
    52→	HTTPDebugRequestHeaders  string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS" envDefault:"none"`
    53→	HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS" envDefault:"Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent"`
    54→	HTTPDebugRedactSensitive bool   `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE" envDefault:"true"`
    55→	HTTPDebugResponseMode    string `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE" envDefault:"none"`
    56→	HTTPDebugBodyMaxBytes    int    `env:"HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES" envDefault:"4096"`
    57→
    58→	// logLevelVar holds the dynamic log level. It is initialized by LogHandler
    59→	// and can be updated at runtime to change the global log level without a restart.
    60→	logLevelVar slog.LevelVar
    61→
    62→	// swappableWriter is the log output target, swappable at runtime.
    63→	swappableWriter *SwappableWriter
    64→
    65→	// categoryHandler wraps the slog handler with per-category level overrides.
    66→	categoryHandler *CategoryHandler
    67→}
    68→
    69→// Load parses and validates configuration from environment variables.
    70→func Load() (*Config, error) {
    71→	cfg := &Config{}
    72→	if err := env.Parse(cfg); err != nil {
    73→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("config: %w", err)
    74→	}
    75→	return cfg, nil
    76→}
    77→
    78→// Addr returns the host:port bind address.
    79→func (c *Config) Addr() string {
    80→	return fmt.Sprintf("%s:%d", c.Host, c.Port)
    81→}
    82→
    83→// LevelTrace is a custom log level below DEBUG for very detailed diagnostic
    84→// output. Usage: logger.Log(ctx, config.LevelTrace, "msg", "key", val)
    85→const LevelTrace = slog.Level(-8)
    86→
    87→// ParseLogLevel converts a log level string to the corresponding slog.Level.
    88→func ParseLogLevel(s string) slog.Level {
    89→	switch s {
    90→	case "trace":
    91→		return LevelTrace
    92→	case "debug":
    93→		return slog.LevelDebug
    94→	case "warn":
    95→		return slog.LevelWarn
    96→	case "error":
    97→		return slog.LevelError
    98→	default:
    99→		return slog.LevelInfo
   100→	}
   101→}
   102→
   103→// LogHandler returns an slog.Handler configured per LOG_LEVEL and LOG_FORMAT.
   104→// The handler uses a dynamic LevelVar so the log level can be changed at
   105→// runtime via LogLevelVar().Set() without recreating the handler. Output is
   106→// routed through a SwappableWriter and wrapped with a CategoryHandler for
   107→// per-category level overrides.
   108→func (c *Config) LogHandler() slog.Handler {
   109→	c.logLevelVar.Set(ParseLogLevel(c.LogLevel))
   110→	c.swappableWriter = NewSwappableWriter()
   111→
   112→	opts := &slog.HandlerOptions{
   113→		Level:     &c.logLevelVar,
   114→		AddSource: c.Env == "development",
   115→		ReplaceAttr: func(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {
   116→			if a.Key == slog.LevelKey {
   117→				if level, ok := a.Value.Any().(slog.Level); ok && level == LevelTrace {
   118→					a.Value = slog.StringValue("TRACE")
   119→				}
   120→			}
   121→			return a
   122→		},
   123→	}
   124→
   125→	var inner slog.Handler
   126→	if c.LogFormat == "text" {
   127→		inner = slog.NewTextHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   128→	} else {
   129→		inner = slog.NewJSONHandler(c.swappableWriter, opts)
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	c.categoryHandler = NewCategoryHandler(inner, &c.logLevelVar)
   133→	return c.categoryHandler
   134→}
   135→
   136→// LogLevelVar returns the dynamic log level variable. Callers can change
   137→// the active log level at runtime by calling Set() on the returned LevelVar.
   138→func (c *Config) LogLevelVar() *slog.LevelVar {
   139→	return &c.logLevelVar
   140→}
   141→
   142→// LogWriter returns the swappable writer used for log output.
   143→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   144→func (c *Config) LogWriter() *SwappableWriter {
   145→	return c.swappableWriter
   146→}
   147→
   148→// LogCategoryHandler returns the category handler for per-category level overrides.
   149→// Returns nil if LogHandler has not been called yet.
   150→func (c *Config) LogCategoryHandler() *CategoryHandler {
   151→	return c.categoryHandler
   152→}
   153→
   154→// HTTPDebugEnabled reports whether any HTTP debug logging is configured.
   155→func (c *Config) HTTPDebugEnabled() bool {
   156→	return c.HTTPDebugRequestHeaders != "none" || c.HTTPDebugResponseMode != "none"
   157→}
   158→
   159→// ParsedSelectedHeaders splits the comma-separated HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders
   160→// into a trimmed slice of header names.
   161→func (c *Config) ParsedSelectedHeaders() []string {
   162→	raw := strings.Split(c.HTTPDebugSelectedHeaders, ",")
   163→	out := make([]string, 0, len(raw))
   164→	for _, h := range raw {
   165→		h = strings.TrimSpace(h)
   166→		if h != "" {
   167→			out = append(out, h)
   168→		}
   169→	}
   170→	return out
   171→}

1→# ─── Kapow API — Environment Variables ────────────────────────────────────────
     2→# Copy this file to .env and fill in real values.
     3→# NEVER commit .env to version control.
     4→
     5→# ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     6→HOST=0.0.0.0
     7→PORT=8081
     8→
     9→# ─── Database ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10→# PostgreSQL connection string (pgx format)
    11→# Required — server will not start without this.
    12→DATABASE_URL=postgres://user:password@localhost:5432/kapow?sslmode=disable
    13→# For `make dev-db-up` (standalone dev database on port 5433):
    14→# DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/kapow?sslmode=disable
    15→
    16→# ─── Authentication (JWT) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17→# HS256 shared secret — used when JWT_PUBLIC_KEY is not set (dev / simple clients)
    18→JWT_SECRET=change-me-to-a-long-random-secret
    19→
    20→# RS256 PEM-encoded public key — if set, overrides JWT_SECRET
    21→# JWT_PUBLIC_KEY=-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----\n...\n-----END PUBLIC KEY-----
    22→
    23→# Expected "iss" claim in incoming tokens (optional)
    24→# JWT_ISSUER=https://auth.example.com
    25→
    26→# Expected "aud" claims — comma-separated (optional)
    27→# JWT_AUDIENCE=kapow-api
    28→
    29→# ── Supabase shortcut ──
    30→# Use the JWT secret from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → JWT Secret:
    31→# JWT_SECRET=<your-supabase-jwt-secret>
    32→# JWT_ISSUER=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co/auth/v1
    33→# JWT_AUDIENCE=authenticated
    34→
    35→# Future: OIDC discovery URL (not yet active)
    36→# OIDC_DISCOVERY_URL=https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration
    37→
    38→# ─── Supabase (Frontend Auth SDK) ───────────────────────────────────────────
    39→# Project URL and anon key from Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API.
    40→# These are used by the Supabase JS SDK in the frontend for login/signup flows.
    41→# SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
    42→# SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
    43→
    44→# Vite exposes these to the browser (VITE_ prefix required)
    45→# VITE_SUPABASE_URL=https://<project-ref>.supabase.co
    46→# VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=eyJ...
    47→
    48→# ─── Logging ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    49→LOG_LEVEL=info          # trace | debug | info | warn | error
    50→LOG_FORMAT=json         # json (default/prod) | text (dev)
    51→
    52→# ─── HTTP Debug Logging ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    53→# Request header logging: none (off) | selected | all
    54→# HTTP_DEBUG_REQUEST_HEADERS=none
    55→# Comma-separated headers to log when mode is "selected"
    56→# HTTP_DEBUG_SELECTED_HEADERS=Content-Type,Accept,User-Agent
    57→# Redact sensitive headers (Authorization, Cookie, Set-Cookie, X-Api-Key)
    58→# HTTP_DEBUG_REDACT_SENSITIVE=true
    59→# Response logging: none (off) | headers | full (headers + body)
    60→# HTTP_DEBUG_RESPONSE_MODE=none
    61→# Max response body bytes to capture when mode is "full"
    62→# HTTP_DEBUG_BODY_MAX_BYTES=4096
    63→
    64→# ─── Environment ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    65→ENV=development         # development | staging | production
    66→
    67→# ─── CORS ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    68→# Comma-separated allowed origins (empty = no CORS headers set)
    69→CORS_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080
    70→
    71→# ─── GraphQL ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72→GRAPHQL_PLAYGROUND=true     # Enable Playground UI (dev only — disable in prod)
    73→GRAPHQL_INTROSPECTION=true  # Enable schema introspection
    74→
    75→# ─── MCP (Model Context Protocol) ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    76→MCP_ENABLED=true               # Enable MCP server endpoint at /mcp
    77→# MCP requires a valid JWT — same tokens used for /graphql work for /mcp.
    78→# OAuth 2.1 Protected Resource Metadata is served at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource
    79→
    80→# ─── OpenTelemetry ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    81→# Set to true to enable OTLP HTTP export of traces and metrics.
    82→OTEL_ENABLED=false
    83→# Standard OTel SDK env vars (only used when OTEL_ENABLED=true):
    84→# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=https://otlp.example.com:4318
    85→# OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_HEADERS=x-api-key=YOUR_KEY
    86→# OTEL_SERVICE_NAME=kapow-api   (overridden by code — set in Config)
    87→# See https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/configuration/sdk-environment-variables/
    88→
    89→# ─── Shutdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    90→SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT=15s
    91→
    92→# ─── Export ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    93→MAX_EXPORT_ROWS=500                # Maximum rows in CSV export (default: 500)
    94→
    95→# ─── Rate Limiting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96→RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true            # Master switch for all rate limits
    97→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL=60              # /graphql requests per minute (anonymous)
    98→RATE_LIMIT_GRAPHQL_AUTH=120        # /graphql requests per minute (authenticated)
    99→RATE_LIMIT_EXPORT=10               # /api/export/csv requests per minute (per user)
   100→RATE_LIMIT_MCP=120                 # /mcp/* requests per minute (per user)
   101→
   102→# ─── Private Endpoint Access ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
   103→# Comma-separated CIDRs allowed to access /private/* routes
   104→PRIVATE_ALLOWLIST_CIDRS=127.0.0.0/8,::1/128

    12→// Claims extends jwt.RegisteredClaims with application-specific fields.
    13→type Claims struct {
    14→	jwt.RegisteredClaims
    15→	Email        string         `json:"email,omitempty"`
    16→	Roles        []string       `json:"roles,omitempty"`
    17→	Role         string         `json:"role,omitempty"`          // Supabase: "authenticated", "anon", etc.
    18→	TeamID       string         `json:"team_id,omitempty"`
    19→	ClientType   string         `json:"client_type,omitempty"`   // web|cli|mobile|desktop
    20→	AppMetadata  map[string]any `json:"app_metadata,omitempty"`  // Supabase: provider info
    21→	UserMetadata map[string]any `json:"user_metadata,omitempty"` // Supabase: user profile data
    22→}
    23→
    24→// NormalizedRoles returns a deduplicated union of Roles and the singular Role field.
    25→func (c *Claims) NormalizedRoles() []string {
    26→	seen := make(map[string]struct{}, len(c.Roles)+1)
    27→	var result []string
    28→	for _, r := range c.Roles {
    29→		if _, ok := seen[r]; !ok {
    30→			seen[r] = struct{}{}

1→schema:

input.prompt:
Implement the frontend feedback form for the Kapow project. The backend GraphQL mutation will be available as:

```graphql
enum FeedbackCategory { BUG  FEATURE  GENERAL }

input SubmitFeedbackInput {
  category: FeedbackCategory!
  subject: String!
  description: String!
  email: String
  userAgent: String
  pageUrl: String
}

type FeedbackResult { id: String!  success: Boolean! }

extend type Mutation {
  submitFeedback(input: SubmitFeedbackInput!): FeedbackResult!
}
```

## Files to create

### 1. `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.tsx`

A Mantine Modal with a form for submitting feedback. Follow the pattern from `web/src/components/auth/PreferencesModal.tsx`.

```tsx
import { useState, useCallback } from 'react'
import { Modal, Stack, Select, TextInput, Textarea, Alert, Group } from '@mantine/core'
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
import { useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'
import { useAuthStore } from '@/stores/authStore'
```

**Component**: `FeedbackModal({ opened, onClose })`

**Mutation** (using gql.tada — MANDATORY):
```tsx
const SubmitFeedbackMutation = graphql(`
  mutation SubmitFeedback($input: SubmitFeedbackInput!) {
    submitFeedback(input: $input) {
      id
      success
    }
  }
`)
```

**Form state**:
- `category`: string | null (required) — options: `[{ value: 'BUG', label: 'Bug Report' }, { value: 'FEATURE', label: 'Feature Request' }, { value: 'GENERAL', label: 'General Feedback' }]`
- `subject`: string (required, max 200 chars)
- `description`: string (required, max 5000 chars)  
- `email`: string (optional, only shown when user is NOT authenticated)
- `submitting`: boolean
- `feedback`: `{ type: 'success' | 'error'; message: string } | null`

**Auth check**: Use `useAuthStore((s) => s.session)` to check if user is logged in. If logged in, don't show the email field.

**Submit handler**:
- Validate: category required, subject required, description required
- Show inline validation errors
- Call the mutation with variables including `userAgent: navigator.userAgent` and `pageUrl: window.location.href`
- On success: show success alert, reset form after a short delay, then close modal
- On error: show error alert

**UI Layout** (Mantine components):
```
Modal title="Send Feedback" size="md"
  Stack gap="md"
    Select label="Category" placeholder="What kind of feedback?" required
    TextInput label="Subject" placeholder="Brief summary" required maxLength={200}
    Textarea label="Description" placeholder="Tell us more..." required maxLength={5000} minRows={4}
    TextInput label="Email" placeholder="your@email.com" (only if !session) description="Optional — so we can follow up"
    
    {feedback alert if present}
    
    Group justify="flex-end"
      Button variant="outline" onClick={onClose}>Cancel</Button>
      Button variant="accent" onClick={handleSubmit} disabled={submitting}>
        {submitting ? 'Sending...' : 'Send Feedback'}
      </Button>
    /Group
  /Stack
/Modal
```

### 2. `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.tsx`

A floating action button that opens the FeedbackModal.

```tsx
import { Affix, ActionIcon, Tooltip } from '@mantine/core'
import { useDisclosure } from '@mantine/hooks'
import { MessageSquare } from 'lucide-react'
import { FeedbackModal } from './FeedbackModal'
```

**Component**: `FeedbackButton()`

Uses Mantine's `Affix` for fixed positioning at bottom-right:
```tsx
<Affix position={{ bottom: 20, right: 20 }}>
  <Tooltip label="Send feedback" position="left">
    <ActionIcon
      size="xl"
      radius="xl"
      variant="filled"
      color="accent"
      onClick={open}
      aria-label="Send feedback"
    >
      <MessageSquare size={20} />
    </ActionIcon>
  </Tooltip>
</Affix>
<FeedbackModal opened={opened} onClose={close} />
```

### 3. `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackButton.test.tsx`

Simple test:
- Import `renderWithMantine` from `@/test/render`
- Test that the button renders
- Test that clicking the button opens the modal (check for modal title "Send Feedback")

Use this pattern:
```tsx
import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest'
import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'
import userEvent from '@testing-library/user-event'
import { renderWithMantine } from '@/test/render'
import { FeedbackButton } from './FeedbackButton'
```

### 4. `web/src/components/feedback/FeedbackModal.test.tsx`

Test the modal component:
- Renders form fields when opened
- Shows email field when not authenticated
- Category, subject, description are required (test validation)
- Mock the urql mutation

Use this pattern for mocking:
```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest'
import { screen } from '@testing-library/react'
import { renderWithMantine } from '@/test/render'
import { FeedbackModal } from './FeedbackModal'

// Mock urql
vi.mock('urql', async () => {
  const actual = await vi.importActual('urql')
  return {
    ...actual,
    useMutation: () => [{ fetching: false, error: null }, vi.fn()],
  }
})
```

### 5. Modify `web/src/App.tsx`

Add the FeedbackButton to the App component. Import and render it after Footer:

```tsx
import { FeedbackButton } from '@/components/feedback/FeedbackButton'

// In the JSX, after <Footer />:
<FeedbackButton />
```

## Important conventions
- Use named exports (not default)
- Use `@/` path alias for imports from src/
- Use Mantine style props, NOT Tailwind
- Use `graphql()` from `gql.tada` for mutations (MANDATORY)
- Use `useMutation` from `@/lib/graphql` (not from urql directly)
- Use brand colors via Mantine color refs: `color="accent"`, `c="brand.9"`
- Use `Button` from `@/components/ui/button` (not from Mantine directly)

## DO NOT run `pnpm build` or `pnpm lint` — I will verify separately.

Working directory is `/home/ketan/project/kapow`. Make all file paths absolute.

content.content:
1→import { useState, useEffect, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { Modal, Stack, Select, Alert, Group, Text } from '@mantine/core'
     3→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'
     4→import { LocationInput, type UserLocationValue } from '@/components/search/LocationInput'
     5→import { US_STATES } from '@/components/search/constants'
     6→import { usePreferencesStore } from '@/stores/preferencesStore'
     7→import { MapPin } from 'lucide-react'
     8→
     9→interface PreferencesModalProps {
    10→  opened: boolean
    11→  onClose: () => void
    12→}
    13→
    14→type FeedbackState = { type: 'success' | 'error'; message: string } | null
    15→
    16→export function PreferencesModal({ opened, onClose }: PreferencesModalProps) {
    17→  const homeLatitude = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeLatitude)
    18→  const homeLongitude = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeLongitude)
    19→  const homeState = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.homeState)
    20→  const loaded = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.loaded)
    21→  const load = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.load)
    22→  const update = usePreferencesStore((s) => s.update)
    23→
    24→  const [location, setLocation] = useState<UserLocationValue | undefined>(undefined)
    25→  const [state, setState] = useState<string | null>(null)
    26→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false)
    27→  const [feedback, setFeedback] = useState<FeedbackState>(null)
    28→
    29→  // Load preferences when modal opens
    30→  useEffect(() => {
    31→    if (opened) {
    32→      void load()
    33→    }
    34→  }, [opened, load])
    35→
    36→  // Sync store values into local state when loaded
    37→  useEffect(() => {
    38→    if (!loaded) return
    39→    if (homeLatitude != null && homeLongitude != null) {
    40→      setLocation({
    41→        latitude: homeLatitude,
    42→        longitude: homeLongitude,
    43→        state: homeState ?? undefined,
    44→      })
    45→    } else {
    46→      setLocation(undefined)
    47→    }
    48→    setState(homeState)
    49→  }, [loaded, homeLatitude, homeLongitude, homeState])
    50→
    51→  // Clear feedback when modal closes
    52→  useEffect(() => {
    53→    if (!opened) setFeedback(null)
    54→  }, [opened])
    55→
    56→  const handleSave = useCallback(async () => {
    57→    setSaving(true)
    58→    setFeedback(null)
    59→    try {
    60→      await update({
    61→        homeLatitude: location?.latitude ?? null,
    62→        homeLongitude: location?.longitude ?? null,
    63→        homeState: state ?? location?.state ?? null,
    64→      })
    65→      setFeedback({ type: 'success', message: 'Preferences saved.' })
    66→    } catch {
    67→      setFeedback({ type: 'error', message: 'Failed to save preferences.' })
    68→    } finally {
    69→      setSaving(false)
    70→    }
    71→  }, [location, state, update])
    72→
    73→  const handleClear = useCallback(async () => {
    74→    setSaving(true)
    75→    setFeedback(null)
    76→    try {
    77→      await update({
    78→        homeLatitude: null,
    79→        homeLongitude: null,
    80→        homeState: null,
    81→      })
    82→      setLocation(undefined)
    83→      setState(null)
    84→      setFeedback({ type: 'success', message: 'Preferences cleared.' })
    85→    } catch {
    86→      setFeedback({ type: 'error', message: 'Failed to clear preferences.' })
    87→    } finally {
    88→      setSaving(false)
    89→    }
    90→  }, [update])
    91→
    92→  const handleLocationChange = useCallback((loc: UserLocationValue | undefined) => {
    93→    setLocation(loc)
    94→    // Auto-set state from location if available and state not manually set
    95→    if (loc?.state && !state) {
    96→      setState(loc.state)
    97→    }
    98→    setFeedback(null)
    99→  }, [state])
   100→
   101→  const hasPreferences = location != null || state != null
   102→
   103→  return (
   104→    <Modal opened={opened} onClose={onClose} title="Preferences" size="sm">
   105→      <Stack gap="md">
   106→        <Stack gap="xs">
   107→          <Text size="sm" fw={500}>
   108→            Home location
   109→          </Text>
   110→          <LocationInput value={location} onChange={handleLocationChange} />
   111→        </Stack>
   112→
   113→        <Select
   114→          label="Home state"
   115→          placeholder="Select a state"
   116→          data={US_STATES}
   117→          value={state}
   118→          onChange={setState}
   119→          searchable
   120→          clearable
   121→        />
   122→
   123→        {feedback && (
   124→          <Alert
   125→            color={feedback.type === 'success' ? 'green' : 'red'}
   126→            variant="light"
   127→          >
   128→            {feedback.message}
   129→          </Alert>
   130→        )}
   131→
   132→        <Group justify="space-between">
   133→          <Button
   134→            variant="light"
   135→            size="xs"
   136→            onClick={() => void handleClear()}
   137→            disabled={saving || !hasPreferences}
   138→          >
   139→            Clear all
   140→          </Button>
   141→          <Group gap="xs">
   142→            <Button variant="outline" size="xs" onClick={onClose}>

1→import { Button as MantineButton, type ButtonProps as MantineButtonProps } from '@mantine/core'
     2→import { forwardRef } from 'react'
     3→
     4→type Variant = 'default' | 'accent' | 'outline' | 'accent-outline' | 'ghost' | 'light' | 'link'
     5→type Size = 'default' | 'xs' | 'compact-xs' | 'sm' | 'lg' | 'xl' | 'icon'
     6→
     7→export interface ButtonProps
     8→  extends Omit<MantineButtonProps, 'variant' | 'color' | 'size'>,
     9→    Omit<React.ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement>, 'color' | 'style'> {
    10→  variant?: Variant
    11→  size?: Size
    12→  asChild?: boolean
    13→}
    14→
    15→const variantMap: Record<Variant, Pick<MantineButtonProps, 'variant' | 'color'>> = {
    16→  default: { variant: 'filled', color: 'brand' },
    17→  accent: { variant: 'filled', color: 'accent' },
    18→  outline: { variant: 'outline', color: 'brand' },
    19→  'accent-outline': { variant: 'outline', color: 'accent' },
    20→  ghost: { variant: 'subtle', color: 'brand' },
    21→  light: { variant: 'light', color: 'brand' },
    22→  link: { variant: 'transparent', color: 'brand' },
    23→}
    24→
    25→const sizeMap: Record<Size, MantineButtonProps['size']> = {
    26→  default: 'md',
    27→  xs: 'xs',
    28→  'compact-xs': 'compact-xs',
    29→  sm: 'sm',
    30→  lg: 'lg',
    31→  xl: 'xl',
    32→  icon: 'md',
    33→}
    34→
    35→const Button = forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement, ButtonProps>(
    36→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    37→  ({ variant = 'default', size = 'default', className, asChild: _asChild, ...props }, ref) => {
    38→    const mapped = variantMap[variant]
    39→    return (
    40→      <MantineButton
    41→        ref={ref}
    42→        variant={mapped.variant}
    43→        color={mapped.color}
    44→        size={sizeMap[size]}
    45→        className={className}
    46→        {...props}
    47→      />
    48→    )
    49→  },
    50→)
    51→Button.displayName = 'Button'

1→/**
     2→ * Strict GraphQL utilities — re-exports urql hooks with types that reject raw strings.
     3→ *
     4→ * Import useQuery/useMutation from here instead of 'urql'. Raw string queries
     5→ * bypass gql.tada's compile-time schema validation, which has caused production
     6→ * bugs (e.g. querying fields that don't exist in the schema).
     7→ *
     8→ * These wrappers require a parsed DocumentNode (has 'kind' and 'definitions'),
     9→ * which gql.tada's graphql() returns but raw strings do not satisfy.
    10→ *
    11→ * Usage:
    12→ *   import { graphql } from 'gql.tada'
    13→ *   import { useQuery, useMutation } from '@/lib/graphql'
    14→ *
    15→ *   const MyQuery = graphql(`query MyQuery { ... }`)
    16→ *   const [result] = useQuery({ query: MyQuery })
    17→ */
    18→
    19→import {
    20→  useQuery as _useQuery,
    21→  useMutation as _useMutation,
    22→  type UseQueryResponse,
    23→  type UseMutationResponse,
    24→  type AnyVariables,
    25→  type OperationContext,
    26→  type RequestPolicy,
    27→} from 'urql'
    28→
    29→// A parsed GraphQL document has { kind: 'Document', definitions: [...] }.
    30→// gql.tada's graphql() returns this at runtime. Raw strings don't have these
    31→// properties, so TypeScript rejects them.
    32→interface ParsedDocument {
    33→  readonly kind: string
    34→  readonly definitions: readonly unknown[]
    35→}
    36→
    37→interface StrictQueryArgs<Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables> {
    38→  query: ParsedDocument
    39→  variables?: Variables
    40→  requestPolicy?: RequestPolicy
    41→  context?: Partial<OperationContext>
    42→  pause?: boolean
    43→}
    44→
    45→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    46→export function useQuery<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    47→  args: StrictQueryArgs<Variables>,
    48→): UseQueryResponse<Data, Variables> {
    49→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    50→  return _useQuery(args as any)
    51→}
    52→
    53→// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    54→export function useMutation<Data = any, Variables extends AnyVariables = AnyVariables>(
    55→  query: ParsedDocument,
    56→): UseMutationResponse<Data, Variables> {
    57→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
    58→  return _useMutation(query as any)
    59→}
    60→

    10→  initialize: () => Promise<void>
    11→  setSession: (session: Session | null) => void

    14→}

    16→export const useAuthStore = create<AuthState>()((set, get) => ({
    17→  session: null,

    20→
    21→  initialize: async () => {

    24→    } = await supabase.auth.getSession()
    25→    set({ session, user: session?.user ?? null, loading: false })
    26→
    27→    supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange(
    28→      (_event: AuthChangeEvent, session: Session | null) => {
    29→        set({ session, user: session?.user ?? null })
    30→      },
    31→    )
    32→  },
    33→
    34→  setSession: (session: Session | null) => {

    36→  },
    37→
    38→  signOut: async () => {
    39→    await supabase.auth.signOut()
    40→    set({ session: null, user: null })
    41→  },
    42→
    43→  getToken: () => {
    44→    return get().session?.access_token
    45→  },
    46→}))
